Three Australian celebrity stylists, Elizabeth Stewart, Cristina Ehrlich, and Jeanann Williams, have made Hollywood Reporter's 'Power Stylists for 2015' list for their work with A-list Aussie stars such as Cate Blanchett, Margot Robbie, and Amanda Seyfried. Stewart, a former fashion editor for Women's Wear Daily and the New York Times, has dressed mega stars including Sandra Bullock and Julia Roberts. Ehrlich is responsible for Margot Robbie's fresh-faced glamour and Isabel Lucas' Amazonian style, while Williams keeps Naomi Watts on the best-dressed lists and manages her partner Liev Schreiber's dapper look.

Mirko "Cro Cop" Filipovic avenged an eight-year-old knockout loss to Gabriel Gonzaga with a third-round victory in Krakow, after a slow start, he dropped Gonzaga with elbows and secured the stoppage. In the co-main event, Jimi Manuwa outpointed Jan Blachowicz, but there were mixed results for other British fighters as Stevie Ray won by second-round stoppage but Joanne Calderwood was submitted in the first round.

The world's last surviving male northern white rhino, Sudan, is under 24-hour armed guard in Kenya to protect him from poachers after his horn was removed as a last resort. The three remaining northern white rhinos, including Sudan and two females, are the only hope for future calves as poaching and habitat loss have pushed the species to the brink of extinction. The keepers, who risk their lives daily, are seeking funds to secure the ranger team's wages and continue their efforts to safeguard the rhinos.

A semi-professional footballer, Jay Hart, was caught having drunken sex with a female supporter in the dugout during a game and was subsequently dismissed by his team, Clitheroe FC. His girlfriend, Bryony Hibbert, has spoken out in defense of his actions and expressed her disappointment towards those who filmed and shared the footage online.

Mexican authorities have launched an investigation into the case of a 14-year-old girl who was mistakenly sent to the US after a judge ruled she was a long-missing daughter based on a superficial resemblance. The girl, Alondra Luna Nunez, was returned to her family in Mexico after DNA testing proved she was not the daughter of the Houston woman who had claimed her. Mexican prosecutors are investigating possible criminal conduct based on media reports and videos that suggest the girl's best interests were not considered during the legal proceedings.

Amol Gupta, a New Yorker, has filed a lawsuit against ZogSports kickball league, alleging he sustained serious injuries, including a broken nose, elbow, and spine, after running into a brick wall during a match due to the organizers setting the foul and base lines too close to the wall. Gupta is suing for unspecified damages.

A tiny three-week-old kitten named Uno formed an unexpected friendship with a full-grown Dalmatian named Louie in Spokane, Washington. In a heartwarming interaction, Uno climbed over Louie and snuggled between his front legs, while Louie nuzzled and licked the kitten, with Louie's mother approvingly watching on. The Pogue family noted that their Dalmatian has a history of being gentle with kittens and welcoming them into the home.

Werder Bremen secured a crucial 1-0 victory over Hamburg SV, with Franco Di Santo scoring the winning penalty and Hamburg's Valon Behrami being sent off. The result kept Bremen in the running for a Europa League spot and left Hamburg in last place. New Hamburg coach Bruno Labbadia suffered a losing return to his former club, as second-placed VfL Wolfsburg prepared to challenge Bayern Munich's lead later that day.

A not-for-profit organization, Get Rid of Sids Project, which had planned seminars featuring anti-vaccination campaigner Sherri Tenpenny, has had its charity status revoked by the Australian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission due to a review into its activities. The organization, led by Brisbane anti-vaccination campaigner Stephanie Messenger, will lose tax concessions and deductible gift recipient status as a result. The decision was made after controversy over Tenpenny's planned speaking tour in Australia resulted in threats of violence.

An eight-year-old HIV-positive boy, known as Kun Kun, who was expelled from his village and labeled a "ticking time bomb" by his own family and community, has been admitted to a specialist school in Linfen, China, where he can receive proper care and education. The authorities contacted the school after his plight gained worldwide attention, highlighting the stigma and discrimination faced by those with HIV in China, particularly in rural areas.

The N-222 road from Peso de Regua to Pinhao in Portugal and the A591 from Kendal to Keswick in the UK have been named the best driving roads in the world and the UK respectively, according to a study by car rental company Avis. The N-222 offers stunning views of the Douro Valley in Portugal, while the A591 provides beautiful vistas of Lake Windermere in the Lake District. Both roads were praised for their balance of bends, acceleration, cruising, and braking, making for an exhilarating driving experience.

John Carver, Newcastle's embattled head coach, insists he is enjoying the challenge of turning the team's season around despite their five-game losing streak and missing key players like Moussa Sissoko, who is suspended for the Tottenham match. Carver remains optimistic and believes he is getting the best out of the squad, urging them to stop the losing streak and win against Tottenham.

Amir Khan has admitted that his next opponent, previously announced as Chris Algieri, has not been officially confirmed, despite a video announcement. Khan is considering other options, including Frankie Gavin, and cannot fight Kell Brook in June due to Ramadan. Brook, who recently returned to the ring, is hoping to face Khan but has been criticized for riding on his name.

Three women have been jailed for hooliganism in Russia after they were filmed twerking in front of a Second World War memorial while performing to music. Two were sentenced to 15 days in prison, while the third received a 10-day sentence. The incident took place at the Malaya Zemlya monument in Novorossiysk and involved six dancers, one of whom was under 16 and avoided punishment. Prosecutors deemed their dance disrespectful to war history.

Neil MacGregor, 68, the director of the British Museum since 2002, announced his retirement at the end of the year after 13 years, citing the completion of the new building and the museum's readiness for a new phase of presenting different histories of the world. The museum, which received 6.7 million visitors in 2014/15, is in great shape with an outstanding team and a collection often referred to as the "memory of mankind."

Jeff Bezos' space company, Blue Origin, has completed work on a new rocket engine for its suborbital spaceship, the New Shepard, which is expected to begin flight tests this year. Blue Origin President Rob Meyerson announced that the BE-3 engine is ready for flight and will take three people to altitudes of about 62 miles above Earth on suborbital journeys from the company's west Texas facility.

A distraught mother, Jaye Cox, and her husband Matthew made the difficult decision to give birth to their sick daughter Annabelle at just 20 weeks due to a serious form of Spina Bifida. After her death, Angel Gowns redesigned Jaye's wedding dress so Annabelle could be buried in it, providing the family with a meaningful and beautiful way to remember their precious daughter.

A Balinese woman, Noor Ellis, admitted to hiring hitmen to murder her Australian husband, Robert Ellis, due to financial and marital issues. Her husband's body was found with wrists and feet bound in a rice field, and she faced court charged with premeditated murder. Her sons, including John Ellis, are now dealing with the loss of both parents.

Olympic gold medalist Nicola Adams was forced to withdraw from the English National Championships in Liverpool after a burglary at her home in Leeds, during which valuable possessions, including training kit, were stolen. Adams expressed deep regret for not being able to compete and thanked everyone for their support. England Boxing understood her decision and wished her well during this difficult time.

Gov. Nathan Deal signed a bill in Georgia to legalize low-THC cannabis oil for certain medical conditions, specifically epilepsy, allowing families like the Coxes to return home with their medication after moving to Colorado for treatment. Haleigh Cox, who had been having hundreds of seizures a day and wasn't responding to five potent drugs, is now thriving after using cannabis oil. The bill, called Haleigh's Hope Act, benefits families who have been separated and suffering from medical conditions.

Tory minister Esther McVey, who has a marginal seat in Wirral West, has condemned Ed Miliband for not speaking out against the 'scurrilous' attacks against her, which include graffiti labeling her a 'murderer' and a song claiming she's going to hell. Local Labour leaders and trade union activists have been behind the campaign, and the vandalism was reported and removed by the council leader. McVey, who was given a job in the Cabinet by David Cameron last July, is one of a few women in the Tory Cabinet and has received support from her female colleagues.

Giant hailstones, some the size of plums, caused damage in the South on Tuesday, with residents capturing the spectacle on camera. The National Weather Service reported over 170 instances of hail, some over two inches in diameter, in Indiana and parts of Mississippi, leading to power outages, traffic delays, and damage to vehicles and property. Christopher Pipkin shared a photo of his mother's car with a smashed windshield.

A 37-year-old man is in custody following the discovery of his wife, 2-year-old daughter, and 8-year-old son dead at their home in suburban Detroit. Police are investigating the circumstances surrounding the deaths and awaiting autopsy results before presenting the case to the prosecutor's office.

Real Madrid manager Carlo Ancelotti remains confident that his team can cope without the injured Luka Modric, Gareth Bale, and Karim Benzema in the second leg of their Champions League quarter-final against Atletico Madrid. He is considering starting Spain international Isco and Jese in their absence and is adamant that Real Madrid's "great" players will rise to the occasion, as they have done in the past. Sergio Ramos and other regulars were put through their paces during training sessions.

Former England cricketer Matt Prior, now CEO of ONE Pro Cycling team, is eager to compete against top cyclists in the inaugural Tour de Yorkshire and aims to elevate his team to pro-continental level. The team's ambition stems from the success of the 2014 Tour de France Grand Depart in Yorkshire, which inspired Prior to give fans a chance to be part of a professional cycling team.

The article discusses how British workers spend an average of £1,840 a year on daily lunchtime takeaways, but could save up to £1,300 a year by preparing lunches at home using simple ideas from the Food52 website. The site offers recipes for simple lunches like cheese and crackers, as well as more complicated options like steamed Chinese buns and Vietnamese banh mi sandwiches. Containers and office microwaves can help keep meals fresh and warm, and the site also provides ideas for vegetarian and exotic lunch options, such as stuffed dumplings and banh mi rolls.

Serena Williams, the twice French Open champion and world No. 1, struggled to beat Sara Errani in the Fed Cup, admitting it was an 'eye-opener' as she was not prepared for the clay court season. The US team was beaten 3-2, with Williams and Alison Riske losing in the doubles, relegating the Americans to World Group II.

The U.N. Security Council passed a resolution on Tuesday imposing an arms embargo on the Houthi rebels and their allies, including former Yemeni President Ali Abdullah Saleh, and implementing sanctions to prevent the spread of terrorism. The council's decision came after weeks of airstrikes led by Saudi Arabia against Houthi targets. Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi and Saudi Arabian Defense Minister Prince Mohammed bin Salman agreed to form a joint military commission to help implement the strategic maneuver inside Saudi Arabia, and Egypt dispatched naval ships to Yemen's waters. CNN reported the news from New York, with contributions from Atlanta and the UN.

Manchester City and Chelsea are both interested in signing Valencia left-back Jose Luis Gaya, who has a £13 million buyout clause. Gaya, 19, is a key player for Valencia and is a Spain Under 21 international, but he is also a target for Manchester City as they consider him a suitable replacement for Aleksandar Kolarov, who is keen to return to Italy.

Veteran actor Robert Hardy, known for his roles in All Creatures Great and Small and as Winston Churchill, is selling his collection of antiques, including a diorama of the Battle of Agincourt, furniture, weapons, and crystal chandeliers, in a £100,000 auction due to downsizing. He will continue to do television work despite retiring from stage acting two years ago.

Russian sailors on board the Admiral Kuznetsov, the largest warship in the Russian Navy, came up with an innovative solution to the time-consuming and hazardous task of manually sweeping the carrier's deck for debris by strapping an old MiG-15 jet engine to a tractor and using it as a powerful leaf-blower to clear the deck before jet planes take off or land.

Arsenal is among the clubs interested in signing Argentine forward Paolo Dybala from Palermo, with the player himself admitting that his last days at the Italian club could be numbered as they prepare to cash in on their star player. Dybala, who has been linked with several top European clubs after impressive performances in Serie A, has reportedly received offers from Arsenal, Juventus, and Liverpool, but denied any discussions with the Reds or a visit to the Nou Camp for transfer talks.

The Green Party leader Natalie Bennett admitted that implementing her party's pledges, including a 'citizens income' and dismantling the Armed Forces, would take longer than five years and could extend 'decades into the future.' She also acknowledged that leaving Nato and abandoning nuclear weapons were long-term aspirations.

A Russian biker gang, the Night Wolves, camped at the Polish border after being banned from entering the country for a World War II memorial ride. The group, described as friends of Vladimir Putin, vowed to enter Poland despite the ban and 15 were seen attempting to cross the border. Polish authorities denied them entry due to their support for Russian actions in Ukraine.

Former Premier League footballer Clarke Carlisle, who attempted suicide by stepping in front of a 12-ton truck in Yorkshire, has revealed he has moved out of the marital home he shared with his wife of 14 years, Gemma. The ex-Burnley defender, who suffered cuts, bruises, internal bleeding, a broken rib, and a shattered left knee in the incident, also apologized to the truck driver for the ordeal but has been unable to meet him in person.

Ed Balls, the shadow chancellor, has dismissed as a 'jokey note' a handwritten note left by former Labour minister Liam Byrne in 2010, which admitted that the party had blown the nation's finances, sparking accusations of contempt towards the public and questioning Labour's credibility on fiscal matters.

Researchers have found that brothers of men convicted of sex offenses are five times more likely than average to commit similar crimes, while sons of fathers with a criminal record for sex offenses are nearly four times more likely. The study, which examined data from over 21,000 offenders in Sweden, suggests that sex offending could run in families along the male genetic line. Notable cases include the Savile brothers, Jimmy and Johnny, and the Jumale brothers, Deeq and Omar. The findings do not mean that a man with a brother or father convicted of rape will inevitably commit the same crime, but genetics may play a role in sexual offending.

Porto, the only undefeated team in the Champions League, will face Bayern Munich in the quarter-finals, with Porto's coach Julen Lopetegui acknowledging there is still room for improvement. In Tuesday's press conference, Pep Guardiola, a two-time Champions League winner, congratulated his former Barcelona teammate Lopetegui on Porto's impressive performances and expressed his excitement for their upcoming clash.

Chinese conglomerate Dalian Wanda Group formally completed the purchase of a 20% stake in Spanish football champions Atletico Madrid at an Extraordinary General Meeting, with Wanda Madrid Investment signing off on the €45million deal. Wanda Group chairman Wang Jianlin expressed excitement about growing Atletico's brand in Asia and leveraging their training expertise to promote football in China.

West Ham manager Sam Allardyce is focused on getting the best performance from his team against Manchester City, who are in a slump but still a formidable opponent. Enner Valencia and Carlton Cole are expected to lead the attack, with Diafra Sakho out due to injury. Allardyce hopes for consistency and a strong finish to the season, with a goal of reaching 50 points or higher.

Florida Wildlife officials warned against throwing baby gopher tortoises in the ocean after at least three reports of people attempting to save them from predators, mistakenly believing them to be baby sea turtles. Gopher tortoises cannot swim and have toes with claws, unlike sea turtles with flippers and one or two claws per fore flipper. The Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission urged people to not interfere with wildlife unless they are professionals, and both gopher tortoises and five species of sea turtles are protected by Florida law.

Researchers at Hadassah Medical School in Jerusalem discovered that pregnancy helps regenerate tissue in older women, with pregnant older mice showing 96% liver regeneration after two days compared to 46% in older non-pregnant mice. Pregnancy's unique condition may act as a "youth serum" for the mother, but the positive effect is transient and lasts about two months after delivery. Previous research suggests that aging may damage brain connections, and filtering youthful blood into older bodies could rejuvenate tissue and improve brain functions.

A trendy double room in Peckham, London, is available for rent for £400 a month, but comes with a leak in the ceiling that the letting agent, 'Martin,' has openly admitted in an online advert. The location is desirable due to its proximity to local amenities and transport into the city, and the flat also features a smart bathroom and kitchen, but the leak is the only major drawback in an otherwise modern and generous space. The affordable price reflects the issue with the ceiling.

During a typical practice session at Creve Coeur Lake outside St. Louis, the Washington University rowing team encountered an unexpected ordeal when a swarm of Asian carp emerged from the water, attacking the boat and even jumping onto the legs of team members Devin Patel and Yoni. Patel screamed for help as the fish slippedperily flopped on his legs, but thankfully no injuries were reported. The incident was captured on video by iReporter Benjamin Rosenbaum for CNN.

Protests in Melbourne and Sydney turned violent as anti-Islam and anti-racism groups clashed, with police forming a wall to separate them. One Twitter account linked to anti-Islam groups mocked protests around Australia, leading to confusion and cancelled events. Two people were arrested in each city and several were injured during the combative protests.

Tickets for the highly-anticipated May 2 boxing match between Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao will go on sale after a ticket allocation agreement was reached, with Pacquiao's promoter, Bob Arum, expressing concerns over the number and location of tickets for Pacquiao and his supporters. Kenny Bayless has been named as the referee for the "fight of the century" at the MGM Grand, with Dave Moretti, Burt Clements, and Glenn Feldman serving as judges. The welterweight world title fight is expected to break revenue records and become the richest bout in boxing history.

Rotherham secured a vital 1-0 win against Brighton, with Matt Derbyshire scoring the only goal after a good cross by Jordan Bowery. The victory moved Rotherham to within two points of Brighton and boosted their survival hopes in the Sky Bet Championship. Leon Best came on as a substitute for Brighton but was unable to prevent the defeat.

An eight-year-old boy from Layton, Utah, named Lota Ward, ran 33 miles in a 50-mile race just before undergoing his fourth brain surgery for a malignant tumor. His love for running, which he had even before his diagnosis, has been a source of strength for him during his treatments.

The annual eVolo Skyscraper Competition recognized the winning designs of Bomp's Essence Skyscraper, Suraksha Bhatla and Sharan Sundar's Shanty-Scraper, and Egor Orlov's Cybertopia, among others, for their innovative ideas for vertical living. Notable entries included a natural habitat skyscraper, a solution for India's slum population, and a city that blurs the lines between digital and physical worlds. Additionally, designs for the Arctic, reversing desertification, and even catching clouds were among the 15 honorable mentions.

Manchester middleweight Michael Bisping was disappointed with his tougher-than-expected victory over CB Dollaway at UFC 186, but was relieved to secure the unanimous decision win for his son Callum's 14th birthday. Bisping, who confirmed his interest in fighting on the upcoming Glasgow card, overcame a rocky first round to secure the decision and prevent Dollaway's takedowns.

A new Ebola vaccine developed in Canada using a live animal virus called vesicular stomatitis virus (VSV) has shown promising results in human trials, inducing an immune response against the Ebola virus and protecting against the disease in primates. The vaccine, which triggers the formation of antibodies against Ebola, is now being administered to people in Guinea and Sierra Leone, where the virus has caused over 10,000 deaths since its outbreak 15 months ago.

Celtic manager Ronny Deila is attempting to persuade Manchester City to allow him to keep defender Jason Denayer on loan for another season, but there is no guarantee Denayer will return to Celtic due to pre-season commitments. Deila is also considering Hearts captain Danny Wilson as a defensive signing, and Manchester City midfielder Liam Henderson has joined Rosenborg on loan until the summer.

John Axford, a pitcher with the Colorado Rockies, has been placed on the family medical emergency list after his 2-year-old son Jameson was bitten by a rattlesnake and required surgery to remove necrotic tissue and combat infection. Axford will be out for at least three days and will travel with his family to Denver for further treatment.

Sweden's Princess Leonore, 14-month-old daughter of Princess Madeleine and British businessman Christopher O'Neill, looked displeased during a visit to the Vatican. The family, including the pregnant Madeleine, met Pope Francis, with O'Neill sharing childcare duties. The royal couple recently returned to Sweden from New York, where Leonore was born, and are expecting their second child this summer. The upcoming wedding of Madeleine's older brother, Prince Carl Philip, adds to their busy schedule. Carl Philip's fiancée, Sofia Hellqvist, a former glamour model, is expected to become Princess Sofia of Sweden following the wedding. Despite controversy surrounding their relationship, Carl Philip's past antics, including drunken rampages and lost wallets, have been a focus of tabloid attention.

The article describes a journalist's experience dining at Washington's Capital Grille, where political deals and assassinations allegedly take place, and their tour of Capitol Hill, home to the Senate and House of Representatives. The author learns about the city's history of power-broking and political manipulation, as well as popular tourist attractions such as the Smithsonian Museum and Ben's Chili Bowl.

Airline rivals Ryanair and Aer Lingus engaged in a playful Twitter feud over their operations at Dublin Airport, with each taking jabs at the other's terminal and destination locations. The latest spat comes as the UK Court of Appeal is set to rule on Ryanair's 29.82% stake in Aer Lingus, with Ryanair actively seeking a buyer for its shares due to competition requirements.

Three people in their 20s and 30s were tragically killed and seven others were critically injured during a stampede outside Santiago's Tsunami nightclub, where British Crust Punk band Doom was performing. According to Dr. Mario Henriquez, the crush caused a structure inside the building to fall, and Police General Hugo Insulza reported several arrests in connection to the tragedy.

A former entertainment reporter for CBS, Kenneth Lombardi, filed a lawsuit against two male colleagues and the network, alleging sexual harassment and discrimination. He claims Duane Tollison, a senior producer, groped and kissed him at a Christmas party in 2013, while Albert Colley, the CBS Evening News Director, came onto him during a drinking session and sent him porn links. Lombardi also accuses HR of failing to address his complaints and his manager of creating a hostile work environment. He resigned in November and is suing for violation of labor laws, emotional distress, and discrimination, including names Arnold Schwarzenegger as a red carpet interviewee he had worked with for CBS New York.

In an open workout session at the Wild Card gym in Los Angeles, Filipino boxing legend Manny Pacquiao shared his motivations for the upcoming May 2 fight against Floyd Mayweather, emphasizing the importance of entertaining fans, giving back to the community, and his belief in the sport of boxing teaching valuable life lessons. Pacquiao, who started fighting for two dollars in the Philippines as a child, wants to speak with Mayweather after the fight about the Gospel of the Lord and the importance of using blessings for good. Mayweather, who sees boxing more as a business, has expressed no interest in a rematch, but Pacquiao hopes for a better fight and an equal split of the purse for a potential second match to help his impoverished countrymen.

This article reveals the little-known history of rebellion and revolution at some of Britain's most prestigious public schools, including Eton and Winchester, which is contrary to their traditional image. The new book "The Old Boys" by David Turner sheds light on tales of masters being held at sword-point, mass walkouts, and battles requiring militia intervention, including gunpowder plots, during the 18th and 19th centuries.

The UK is experiencing its hottest April on record, with temperatures reaching up to 16.6°C in Scotland and Wales. Thousands headed to beaches and parks to enjoy the sunny weather, as forecasters predict a continuation of the warm spell. The article includes several images of people sunbathing, including two women.

Caster Semenya, the former 800-meter world champion and 2012 Olympic silver medalist, won the South African title on Saturday, aiming to qualify for the upcoming World Championships. Her winning time was over four seconds off the qualifying mark, but she has been injury-prone over the last three years and is focusing on staying healthy under new coach Jean Verster, after splitting from former coach Maria Mutola.

Blackpool striker Nile Ranger, 24, has criticized the club for giving him a low-paid contract that left him struggling to afford food, leading him to go AWOL since early December. Despite disciplinary issues in his past with Swindon and Newcastle, he aims to start anew next season.

An Arizona woman, Kym Ackerman, was surprised to find what she believes is the image of Jesus Christ in a dental x-ray of one of her molars during a check-up. The hygienist agreed, but the dentist was less convinced. This is not the first reported instance of Jesus appearing in x-rays; in 2010, a cancer-stricken Indiana woman saw an image of Christ in a chest x-ray taken during her hospitalization for pneumonia.

An elderly golfer was bitten by a crocodile on the leg while playing at the Palmer Sea Reef Golf Course in Port Douglas, Queensland. The man, reportedly in his 70s, received deep lacerations to his shin and puncture wounds to his calf. Paramedics treated him at the scene before taking him to the hospital in a stable condition. Clive Palmer, the course owner, advised people to avoid stepping on crocodiles and wished the victim a speedy recovery. The incident marks the first time a crocodile has been removed from the golf course in the region.

Aston Villa owner Randy Lerner, who missed the team's FA Cup semifinal victory over Liverpool due to a family bereavement, is expected to attend the final against Arsenal. Villa CEO Tom Fox expressed his boss's delight and disappointment, stating that the win could lift the negativity surrounding the club and boost morale for the upcoming final.

Gunshots were fired at rapper Lil Wayne's tour buses in Atlanta early Sunday, leaving no injuries but prompting a police investigation. Witnesses reported seeing a Corvette style vehicle and an SUV at the scene, where two buses were located with multiple bullet holes. The incident occurred on Interstate 285 near Interstate 75 during Lil Wayne's visit to Compound nightclub Saturday night. CNN's Carma Hassan contributed to the report.

Micah Richards, whose contract with Manchester City expires at the end of the season, is uncertain about his future as he faces being a free agent. Despite enjoying his loan spell at Fiorentina, the defender is eager to return to the Premier League and prove himself once again, with Aston Villa, Southampton, Everton, and West Ham reportedly interested. Richards, who has experience playing both as a right-back and central defender, seeks regular playing time and a mainstay role at his next club.

Five Saudi Arabian Airlines baggage handlers have been fired after a video went viral showing them carelessly tossing luggage onto a conveyor belt, causing some to hit a green barrier or land with heavy thuds. Passengers and onlookers were shocked and some expressed doubt that other airlines handle baggage any better.

A professor testifying in a California trial claimed she was intimidated by her ex-boyfriend, Gianni Van, into identifying her rapist, Gonzalo Ramirez, who was later killed by Van and his associates in 1995. The professor, Norma Esparza, was 'broken' by the assault and feared for her safety when Van demanded she point out Ramirez at a bar.

A discovery in Poitou-Charentes, France, of fossils dating back 57,600 years revealed Neanderthals may have practiced cannibalism, as indicated by cuts and fractures on the bones of two adults and a child, potentially for food or funeral rituals. The site also contained remains of animals, suggesting Neanderthals used it as a hunting area.

Chelsea have initiated contract negotiations with Patrick Bamford following his impressive loan spell at Middlesbrough, where he has scored 17 goals and helped the team in their promotion push. The English striker, currently on a £7,500 weekly wage, has earned a new deal offer from Chelsea with over a year remaining on his current contract.

A new enigmatic riddle game called None* offers users only one clue to get started and a keyboard to type their guesses, with no images, music, animations, or hints to aid in solving the puzzles. Available for iOS, Android, and Windows Phone devices, the free app has 50 levels and has been completed by around 10% of its users, who are eagerly anticipating the release of Episode 2. Some find the game enjoyable, while others find it frustratingly vague.

An 8-year-old boy, Brock Guzman, was kidnapped while asleep in the backseat of his family's stolen 2001 Toyota Corolla in Northern California. Authorities issued an Amber Alert and conducted a frantic search, but found the boy safe and unharmed in the abandoned vehicle about two miles away from home. The thief likely didn't notice the boy asleep in the backseat during the crime of opportunity.

Adam Lyons and his girlfriends Brooke Shedd and Jane Shalakhova appeared on This Morning to discuss their polyamorous relationship, where they all share a life and a bed without jealousy, and explained that they don't view it as sharing Adam but as a unique situation where they all love each other.

Crystal Palace manager Alan Pardew expresses confidence in his ability to manage one of England's top clubs, but is content at Selhurst Park for now. He believes his team's teamwork can overcome Manchester City's technical ability in their upcoming match, despite City's stronger bench options.

CNN Student News reported on the ongoing drought in California and its potential national impacts, as well as a flu outbreak in Chicago affecting animals, and a proposed tunnel connecting Denmark and Germany via an undersea road and rail line. The team producing CNN Student News considers Common Core State Standards and national and state standards when creating the program. To be mentioned on the next CNN Student News, teachers or students aged 13 or older can leave a comment with their school name, mascot, city, and state.

Embarrassing footage has emerged of a failed S300 missile launch in Russia, where the rocket barely lifted off before plummeting back to the ground, resulting in a small explosion and debris. Vladimir Putin, who has increased the Russian defense budget by 33%, may be embarrassed by this incident following the recent failure of another rocket launch that killed six technicians.

Baltimore Police Commissioner Anthony Batts admitted mistakes were made in the arrest of Freddie Gray, who died a week after being taken into custody. Gray was not given medical attention when he indicated he was having difficulty breathing, and he was not buckled into a seat belt during transportation. Investigators are looking into the circumstances of each stop the van made en route to the station.

At a Labour party rally in Warrington, comedians Ben Elton and Eddie Izzard, along with Coronation Street actress Sally Lindsay and reality TV star Joey Essex, spoke in support of the party. Elton criticized Myleene Klass for her opposition to Ed Miliband's mansion tax plans, while Miliband announced a £5 billion Future Homes Fund to build 125,000 new homes.

Princess Eugenie, 25, currently living in New York for work, was spotted in the city wearing affordable pieces from Topshop and Intermix, including a striped miniskirt and a Zara biker coat. She completed her urban chic look with Chelsea boots and a Rebecca Minkoff handbag. The young royal recently celebrated her birthday with a sing-along at a Palace event, where her father, Prince Andrew, organized a surprise rendition of "Happy Birthday" via FaceTime.

An unusually resistant strain of Shigella bacteria, known as "Delhi Belly" or "Montezuma's revenge," has caused over 240 illnesses in 32 states and Puerto Rico since last May, with nearly 90% of cases being resistant to the antibiotic ciprofloxacin. The superbug, which has re-infected people who had previously been immune, was traced to travelers returning from the Dominican Republic, India, and other countries. Shigella causes diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea and is estimated to cause 500,000 cases in the US annually. The CDC is concerned about the troubling trend of drug-resistant shigellosis outbreaks in the US.

A man named Craig Lister, 54, who is undergoing hormone therapy for advanced prostate cancer that has spread beyond his pelvis, experiences frequent hot flushes as a side-effect. The therapy, which acts on the brain to reduce testosterone production and bind with cancer cells to prevent growth, can cause various side-effects including fatigue, loss of libido, mood swings, depression, breast swelling, and weight gain. The therapy also raises the risk of cardiovascular disease and type 2 diabetes, and can cause bone thinning. Craig, who lives with his wife and three children, including Joseph, has taken to carrying a spare shirt to meetings due to the hot flushes. A recent study suggests that cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), a psychological technique, may help alleviate hot flushes and night sweats in men undergoing hormone therapy for prostate cancer.

A former air stewardess, Gemma Flanagan, 31, from Liverpool, who was left paralyzed by Guillain Barre Syndrome after assuming her leg pain from partying in six-inch heels was just jet lag, makes her catwalk debut today in a wheelchair at London's Olympia for the 'The Beauty Within' show by Models of Diversity.

A woman named Roxy Walsh, who found a sentimental gold ring with the inscription "Love Jenny" while snorkeling in Bali, has successfully reunited it with its owner, a couple named Joe and Jenny, after an overwhelming response on social media. The ring, which was lost and believed to be from the Sunshine Coast in Queensland, was discovered at Finns Beach Club and will be returned to the couple this weekend. Ms. Walsh set up a Facebook page to help find the owners and received an outpouring of support from over 4,000 users.

Olympique Lyonnais dominated Bastia in a Ligue 1 match at Stade de Gerland, with second-half goals from Alexandre Lacazette and substitute Mohamed Yattara securing a 2-0 victory and moving Lyon to the top of the table with 64 points. Lyon were made to wait by Bastia, who were reeling from their recent 4-0 League Cup final defeat to Paris Saint-Germain, but Alphonse Areola's impressive saves kept Lyon at bay until Lacazette and Yattara capitalized on substitutions and Grenier's pass.

In 1961, a cake-factory worker from Castleford named Viv Nicholson defied the advice of a grey-suited financial adviser and spent her entire £152,319 winnings from the pools on extravagant clothes, a pink Cadillac, and wild parties, leaving her and her coal miner husband, Keith, penniless within a few years.

Arsenal defender Calum Chambers has had a successful first season at the Emirates, making 36 appearances and establishing himself as a first team regular. He trained as a central defender during pre-season and made his debut in that position, but is determined to secure a long-term place in central defence and not be known as a utility player. Chambers, who has represented England at the Under-21 level, recently trained with the team in the Czech Republic.

The news of Jurgen Klopp's departure from Borussia Dortmund has increased the likelihood of key players, including Mats Hummels, Ilkay Gundogan, Ciro Immobile, and Neven Subotic, leaving the team this summer. Previous signings of Mario Gotze and Robert Lewandowski by Bayern Munich contributed to Dortmund's decline, and Klopp's exit could mark the end of an era. Hummels is most likely to join Manchester United, while Gundogan is a target for several Premier League clubs, and Immobile may return to Italy. Subotic has expressed interest in playing in a more physical league and has been linked with Manchester United and Arsenal.

Wardens at Windsor Castle, who meet and greet visitors, conduct security searches, and tell the history of the Castle, have voted for industrial action due to low wages and being asked to perform extra duties, such as leading paid-tours and speaking foreign languages, for no additional compensation. The dispute is not with the Queen and Royal Family, but with the managers who run the Castle on their behalf. Staff are considering refusing to host tours, conducting more thorough security searches, and observing every check to cause disruption. The Royal Collection Trust, which employs the wardens, said the outcome of the ballot is disappointing but will have no impact on visitors. The staff's wages were recently raised to the living wage, but they are demanding back-pay for the wage increase that occurred last November.

Three former England rugby internationals, Lewis Moody, Josh Lewsey, and Danny Grewcock, continued with their charity expedition to the North Pole after their plane's landing gear collapsed during takeoff. They were later taken by helicopter to the exact latitude of 89 North and trekked to the pole, enduring temperatures as low as -50C and crossing ice floes and five-feet high ridges. Despite the plane incident, they safely reached their goal and hope to raise £250,000 for the Lewis Moody Foundation and the Royal Marines Charitable Trust.

The article previews the upcoming football match between Arsenal and Liverpool, highlighting key matchups including Olivier Giroud against Kolo Toure and Mamadou Sakho, Per Mertesacker versus Daniel Sturridge, and Aaron Ramsey against Jordan Henderson. With Martin Skrtel suspended, Dejan Lovren may fill in for Liverpool's defense against Arsenal's in-form striker Giroud.

In the Ferguson, Missouri city election, two African-American candidates, Ella Jones and Wesley Bell, won their wards to make up half of the new City Council, marking a significant shift from the predominantly white old council. Both candidates emphasized community policing and making a positive change in Ferguson, with Bell intending to be deeply involved in hiring a new police chief following the resignation of Tommy Jackson and the scathing U.S. Department of Justice report on systemic discrimination. Brian Fletcher, a former mayor, also won a seat, despite criticism for being part of the old guard. The city is now required to approve a new budget without relying on revenue from traffic tickets and fines, which amounted to $3 million or more.

A new survey by Ipsos Mori for BBC Scotland revealed that Scottish voters prefer a referendum on the UK's EU membership over another independence vote. Scottish First Minister Nicola Sturgeon, who has criticized David Cameron's pledge for an EU referendum, refused to rule out another independence vote if the SNP wins the 2016 Holyrood elections.

This article counts down the 20 most legendary shots in Masters history, from Arnold Palmer's final-round birdie on 18 in 1960 to Tiger Woods' iconic chip-in on 16 in 2005. Notable moments include Sandy Lyle's remarkable bunker shot in 1988, Jack Nicklaus' miraculous putt on 16 in 1975, and Bubba Watson's impossible wedge shot out of the trees in 2012. Each shot demonstrates the skill, determination, and excitement that has made the Masters one of golf's most prestigious tournaments.

Bad weather, including tornadoes and thunderstorms, is forecast to impact multiple states over the weekend, with severe thunderstorms predicted for major cities such as Dallas and Houston. The Detroit Tigers played through snow during a game against the New York Yankees on Wednesday.

In this article, Sportsmail's Racing Correspondent Marcus Townend and Britain's top tipster Sam Turner preview the Grand National at Aintree, where a fairytale victory for favorite Shutthefrontdoor could cause 70,000 spectators to celebrate, while the paddock houses a 39-strong field for the race. The duo shares their predictions for the event. (Note: Comments are currently not being accepted on this article.)

Marouane Fellaini paid tribute to departing Liverpool midfielder Steven Gerrard, naming him as the best passer he's faced in his career during an interview with manutd.com. Fellaini also acknowledged Everton's Phil Jagielka as the toughest tackler he's encountered since leaving Goodison Park and named Sylvain Distin as the most athletic player he's played alongside. Gerrard was dismissed just 38 seconds after coming on during Liverpool's defeat to United last month, and Fellaini played against him unanimously for his passing ability, which has been revered by many throughout his career.

Australian Prime Minister Tony Abbott called on EU leaders to follow Australia's tough immigration controls, including processing asylum claims on naval warships and returning rejected applicants home, as over 900 migrants died in the Mediterranean. Italy's prime minister Matteo Renzi labeled migrant traffickers as "slave traders." In a more controversial move, Australia rejected asylum claims of 46 Vietnamese aboard an Australian navy vessel without setting foot on land before sending them back to Vietnam.

An Indian man, later identified as Boobesh Palani, who was rescued from Wellington Harbour by two brave teenage girls has passed away in New Zealand. Police appealed to the public for help in identifying him, and his name was eventually confirmed by a New Zealander who recognized him from a photo online. Palani was studying in New Zealand when he was rescued from shark-infested waters at Burdan's Gate, Eastbourne, by the teenagers.

A man named Steve O'Shaughnessy, 23, killed his partner LaTasha Gosling, 27, and her three young children: Janyaa, 4, Jenika, 8, and Landen, 7, in a grisly murder-suicide. One friend revealed that O'Shaughnessy sent pictures of the bodies as a 'birthday gift' to the children's father, Jason Gosling, the day before the tragic event. Amanda Henderson, a friend of LaTasha, stated that she had ended her relationship with O'Shaughnessy due to his infidelity.

Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi are set to compete for the top scorer title in the Champions League, with eight goals each, but Luiz Adriano of Shakhtar Donetsk threatens to take the spotlight with nine goals, making history with a five-goal performance. Arsenal and Liverpool are reportedly interested in Adriano, who is out of contract at the end of the year, while Luis Suarez and Neymar of Barcelona, Karim Benzema of Real Madrid, and Carlos Tevez of Juventus each have six goals. The draw for the semi-finals will take place on Friday.

The England cricket team's involvement with Allen Stanford, who was later sentenced to 110 years in prison for multi-billion-pound fraud, was defended by Sir Curtly Ambrose and other West Indies cricket legends Viv Richards and Richie Richardson. They accompanied Stanford when he launched his five-year deal with England by helicopter in 2008, despite feeling that having the same opponents over a long period would make things stale. The England Cricket Board's president Giles Clarke, who negotiated the deal, has since remained silent on the matter.

SNP leader Nicola Sturgeon has refused to rule out holding a second Scottish independence referendum before 2020, contradicting previous statements that it was a once-in-a-generation event, prompting accusations of broken promises from opposition leaders.

According to a new report, Georgina Chapman, the wife of Harvey Weinstein, is furious and humiliated by the allegations that he groped a 22-year-old Italian model, and wants to find a resolution as soon as possible to avoid further embarrassment for her children and her business. A socialite source claims Chapman, who has two children with Weinstein, is someone who has "loved Harry through his charms and flaws." Weinstein is currently waiting for the Manhattan district attorney to decide whether to seek charges before making his next move. The couple's representative denies the marriage is in trouble and that the allegations are causing a strain on their relationship.

Madonna shocked the music festival Coachella by French kissing a visibly uncomfortable Drake during her performance, despite reports suggesting he enjoyed the experience due to her lipstick. The age difference and Madonna's reputation as a sex symbol may have contributed to Drake's reaction.

A $16 million community center and apartment complex under construction in Baltimore's East Baltimore neighborhood, intended to provide essential services and affordable housing, was destroyed by fire in the wake of Freddie Gray's funeral. Local clergy described the area as plagued by high unemployment, poverty, and despair, and expressed hope that the center's destruction would not be the community's lasting legacy.

A finishing school called Le Femme, located in New Jersey, has opened for transgender women and cross-dressers, providing guidance on moving, dressing, and speaking like a lady. Founded by Ellen Weirich, the school offers lessons on posture, makeup application, and dressing according to body shape, as well as complete makeovers and outings to practice new skills.

Three main party education spokesmen, including a privately educated Education Secretary and a Labour shadow education secretary, revealed they would consider sending their children to private schools, contrasting Labour leader Ed Miliband's stance on sending his deputy Scottish Leader's son to a private school. Labour pledged to sack thousands of 'unqualified' teachers if they hadn't signed up for training by the end of their first term in government.

Serena Williams, currently ranked No. 1 in the world, heads into the Miami Open final with a perfect 17-0 record in 2015, having overcome a challenging semi-final match against Simona Halep. Despite not playing at her best, Williams remains unbeaten and is set to extend her winning streak into the clay court season, with Halep being the closest contender to challenging her dominance.

A mother from Hull, East Yorkshire, was taken away in a police van and given a caution for theft after she took home a £20 Tesco clothes horse she found on a train, believing it had been abandoned. The rightful owner was on the train at the time and reported the item missing.

A married couple, Derek Lowe, 38, and Tina Lowe, 33, were tragically killed by an Amtrak train in Durham, North Carolina on Easter Sunday. The incident occurred on property owned by Norfolk-Southern, and the train, headed from Charlotte to New York City, continued on its route after a delay of nearly three hours. The couple may have been homeless, according to a witness.

Godfather actor Al Pacino is offering die-hard fans the chance to fly with him on a private jet from Glasgow to London for £25,000 as part of a VIP ticket package for his speaking tour in the UK. The unusual experience includes a ticket to Pacino's show in Glasgow, three nights in a five-star hotel in London, and a chance to fly with the actor, providing fans with an unforgettable opportunity to meet and engage with the icon.

Tony Blair, the former Prime Minister of the UK, faced accusations of megalomania after expressing his intention to set up a 'leaders club' of ex-statesmen, suggesting that effective government is more important than democracy, and praising authoritarian regimes. In an interview with Newsweek, he also shared his plans to make a difference on a global level and work with other former leaders. Conservative MP Andrew Bridgen criticized Blair for his remarks, labeling him a 'megalomaniac.'

Joe Root, left out of the Sydney Test last year, has shown great resilience and talent, producing a series of big innings to solidify his place in the England team. Graham Thorpe's prediction of Root as a rising star was proven correct, as the young batsman's grit and determination have earned him the title of a quality Test batsman.

Patties Foods asserts that its viral testing found no link between its Nanna's brand frozen mixed berries (containing raspberries, blackberries, strawberries from China, and blueberries from Chile) and a national hepatitis A outbreak. The CEO, Steven Chaur, announced that non-recalled batches of Nanna's and Creative Gourmet berries have tested negative for HAV and E.coli and are being returned to supermarkets, while investigations into the source of the outbreak continue. Additionally, several hospitals were advised to discard chocolate mousse contaminated with listeria.

Roberto Carlos, the former Real Madrid defender and 2002 World Cup winner with Brazil, has revealed his ambition to manage the national team despite a poor start with Turkish clubs Sivasspor and Akhisar Belediyespor. He finds coaching "very easy" due to his experience as a player under various top managers, including Fabio Capello and Roy Hodgson.

Police conducted a new search near a Ford Motor Company plant in Michigan for evidence related to the six-month-old disappearance of Chelsea Ellen Bruck, a 22-year-old woman last seen at a Halloween party wearing a Poison Ivy costume.

A motorist in Illinois had a close encounter with a tornado while on the road, filming it from his truck as it passed just a few hundred yards in front of him. Despite feeling trapped and unsure of which direction to escape, he remained calm and continued filming until the tornado passed, leaving him unharmed but warning others against seeking shelter under bridges during tornados as per safety guidelines.

A Bo'ness man, Mr. Dunning, won £7.8 million in the Lotto and reported for his 4am shift at Caledonia Produce the next day to hand in his notice, keeping the winning ticket in his wallet during the shift before being informed by his partner's friend. Their initial plans include purchasing a new house and upgrading their car.

Rickie Lambert may consider suing Liverpool for restraint of trade after being underutilized by manager Brendan Rodgers, who preferred Mario Balotelli despite Lambert's better performance statistics and goal-scoring record for Southampton and Liverpool. Lambert's career has stalled since joining Liverpool, and he missed out on opportunities to play due to injuries and Rodgers' preference for Balotelli, who has failed to deliver for the team.

On April 12, 1945, at the Chicago Daily News, city desk editor Clem Lane and his team sprang into action after receiving a shocking AP bulletin that President Franklin D. Roosevelt had died. The team worked frantically to produce EXTRAs for the afternoon newspaper, with Bob Lewin writing the first story on Harry S. Truman's presidency. As the team worked, they discussed the uncertainty of what kind of president Truman would be, and chief editorial writer Lloyd Lewis reflected on Roosevelt's legacy.

Swansea manager Garry Monk aims to end Swansea's post-international break jinx and claim a much-needed point against Hull, as they look to surpass their best-ever Premier League points total. Despite tough away fixtures against Chelsea, Stoke, and Manchester City, Monk is optimistic about their chances, citing unlucky results and the importance of focusing on their own agenda during the business end of the season. Hull, who have been struggling at the bottom of the table, will be determined to secure a win.

Former Chelsea goalkeeper Carlo Cudicini believes Arsenal should pursue a move for Petr Cech if the Czech Republic international leaves Stamford Bridge this summer, as Arsene Wenger looks for a replacement for Wojciech Szczesny. Cudicini feels Cech, who has fallen behind Thibaut Courtois in the pecking order at Chelsea, would be a good fit at Arsenal.

A Manhattan judge sentenced banana mogul Thomas Hoey Jr. to 12.5 years in prison for distributing cocaine at a sex party where a woman, Kimberly Calo, died from a lethal mix of cocaine and alcohol. Hoey ignored Calo's seizures, prevented others from calling for help, and continued to supply cocaine to friends after her death, obstructing justice.

A family was shocked and disturbed when a KFC restaurant in Oklahoma broadcast a steamy sex scene from the TV show "Outlander" during their dinner, exposing their young children to strong sexual content. KFC apologized and promised to prevent such incidents in the future by ensuring certain channels will no longer be accessible.

In a $1 billion lawsuit, over 750 plaintiffs, including victims and their families, are suing Johns Hopkins University for approving and helping to plan a medical experiment in Guatemala from 1945 to 1956, during which approximately 700 individuals, mostly orphans, were deliberately infected with syphilis. Many victims suffered from open sores covering their bodies, passed the disease down to their children, resulting in brain damage or death.

In a heated exchange on live television, Labour's Harriet Harman and Tory Chancellor George Osborne, both alumni of the same expensive private school, clashed over class war attacks and the Tories' commitment to spending on the NHS. Osborne dismissed Harman's accusations of being 'posh boys,' while Harman defended her criticism of the Tories and their policies.

Thibaut Courtois, the 22-year-old Chelsea goalkeeper, has established himself as one of the best in the world after impressing at Genk, Atletico Madrid, and Chelsea. He has won multiple trophies including the Premier League title, La Liga, and Europa League, and is motivated to add the Premier League title to his collection. Despite some recent slip-ups, Courtois has proven his worth with crucial saves and a fine display in a recent win against QPR.

A six-year-old girl was sexually assaulted by a classmate at her school in Blackburn, but authorities cannot take action as the age of criminal responsibility in England is ten. The girl's mother has since moved her to a new school and is calling for a change in the law.

Sainsbury's is set to launch a new app that allows customers to scan household items at home and navigate the store to find the best prices and routes, with real-time updates on current prices and automatic payments. The app, an extension of Sainsbury's Scan-and-Go scheme, is currently being trialled in Wandsworth and is expected to be rolled out next year.

Lionel Messi is expected to play in Barcelona's La Liga game against Celta Vigo on Sunday despite a foot injury he suffered during the 'Clasico' against Real Madrid last month. Barcelona, who are on a seven-match winning streak and have reached the Champions League quarter-finals and King's Cup final, aim to win major titles this season and face Athletic Bilbao in the latter competition's final.

A Ferrari driver suffered no injuries but seriously injured a male passenger after crashing an uninsured, newly-purchased Ferrari 348 worth between $90k and $110k into a tree in Adelaide's North Brighton suburb. The passenger sustained head and leg injuries, and speed was suspected to be a factor.

Campaigners are urging the government to implement a 90-year-old law that would fix the date of Easter between April 9 and 15 to help businesses plan and boost the economy. The Easter Act 1928, which has never been implemented due to objections from churches, could allow for consistent school holidays and shopping hours on Easter Sunday.

A giant Suffolk Cross lamb named Hagrid, born in Gelston, Lincolnshire, weighing 25lbs and standing 14 inches tall, is believed to be the biggest ever lamb born in the UK. Hagrid, aptly named after the fictional giant in Harry Potter, was born naturally on Tuesday and is making a good recovery with his mother. The video shows the remarkable size of the lamb towering over its peers. Previously, a 24lbs lamb held the record for the largest lamb in the UK, but Hagrid is 2oz heavier.

Piers Morgan, a vocal Arsenal critic and outspoken commentator, urged the football club to sack Arsene Wenger and replace him with Borussia Dortmund's Jurgen Klopp, who announced his departure from the German team and is expected to be available in June. Morgan believes Klopp's winning mentality and success at Dortmund make him the ideal candidate for Arsenal, who are looking for a new manager to lead them to trophies.

A nine-year-old boy, William Kulk, and his eight-year-old sister, Piper, have died from injuries sustained in a car crash on New South Wales' Central Coast. Their mother, Chantelle Boddy, and elder brother Daniel, 12, were also injured when their Holden Astra aquaplaned and collided with a ute, leaving all passengers trapped in the wreckage. Piper died in hospital on Sunday morning, while William died later that evening. Their mother and grandmother sustained fractures and are in stable condition.

A chartered surveyor, Timothy Norris-Jones, was sentenced to 20 months in jail, suspended for two years, for assaulting his 92-year-old mother, leaving her with bruises on her face and a cut on her wrist that required stitches. The incident occurred while her live-in carer was out, and Norris-Jones was banned from spending any time alone with his mother or visiting her house. The assault was described as "horrific" by the judge, who also ordered Norris-Jones to wear an electronic ankle bracelet enforcing a strict curfew.

Manchester United's Ander Herrera has scored six of his seven goals for the team without looking at the ball or the opposition's net, including against Swansea, Leicester, Preston, and Aston Villa, despite being praised for his vision and shooting ability by manager Louis van Gaal. The Spanish midfielder scored a double against Villa with his eyes closed.

The USA women's soccer team unveiled their new black and white kits for the World Cup, produced by Nike, causing some controversy due to the departure from traditional red, white, and blue. Nike explained the focus on white for the home kit, and the new design features fully integrated neon yellow socks and blue cleats. The team kicks off their campaign against Australia on June 8.

George Osborne and David Cameron have pledged to help half a million first-time buyers get on the housing ladder every year by 2020 through government schemes and home building reforms, aiming to reverse a decline in home ownership and double the number of people buying their first home since 2010. Labour's Shadow housing minister criticized the Tory-led government for low levels of housebuilding and declining home ownership during their tenure.

Adrian Heath, manager of Orlando City in the MLS, humorously dismisses the idea of raising the crossbar height to score more goals for American fans, citing soccer's long history and global popularity. He discusses the growth of the MLS, the impact of high-profile signings like Frank Lampard and Steven Gerrard, and the humility of Kaka. He also anticipates a potential rivalry with David Beckham's Miami project and reflects on his own journey from the bottom of the US soccer pyramid to Orlando's success.

A group of doctors have criticized Dr. Mehmet Oz for promoting "quack treatments" and holding a faculty position at Columbia University, accusing him of a "lack of integrity" and "disdain for science." The university has defended Oz's academic freedom to make public statements.

In England, over 50,000 young people have signed up for a new government scheme offering discounted starter homes, as part of an extension to the Help to Buy scheme that aims to provide 200,000 such properties at a 20% discount, helping those desperate to get on the housing ladder. Prime Minister David Cameron reiterated his commitment to increasing home ownership, believing it provides independence and a symbol of success, while critics argue the government's record on the issue is lacking.

England capitalized on early luck, removing both West Indies openers, Kraigg Brathwaite and Devon Smith, despite questionable decisions. Devon Smith was given out caught behind despite clear evidence from replays that he had missed the ball, as HotSpot technology was not available for reviews in this Test series. The West Indies reached 36 for two at lunch, with 11 overs lost due to rain.

In Farnham, Surrey, rubbish teams refused to empty recycling bins due to non-recyclable items like a crisp packet or cellophane on the lid, leaving homeowners with full bins and angering the community, who labeled the teams as "little Hitlers." The council defended the teams, stating they cannot collect contaminated bins to prevent landfill waste.

Manu Tuilagi, England's center, is undergoing weekly rehabilitation from a groin injury that has kept him out of action since October. He aims to return in time for England's pre-World Cup preparations in the summer. Tuilagi missed the autumn internationals and the entire RBS 6 Nations campaign, and his absence allowed Bath's Jonathan Joseph to excel at outside center for England.

The article discusses the character Hodor from Game of Thrones, who only speaks the word "Hodor," and how he may have expressive aphasia. The article also mentions that Hodor's real name is Walder and that his character may be based on a historical case of a man named Tan, who also had expressive aphasia and could only speak one word. The article also mentions that brain damage, such as that caused by epileptic seizures, can lead to expressive aphasia.

The article expresses disappointment towards Arsenal's lack of Premier League titles in the last 11 years, criticizing Arsene Wenger for failing to deliver under pressure and pointing out key losses against Tottenham and Monaco in the Champions League. The author argues that Arsenal's impressive end-of-season form is a result of reduced pressure, but they need to improve in high-pressure situations to be serious title contenders.

London Welsh have announced that former England international Olly Barkley, 33, has signed a new contract to remain with the relegated Aviva Premiership club, despite finishing bottom of the league last season. Barkley, who previously played for Bath, Gloucester, Racing Metro, and Grenoble, won 23 Test caps for England, and is committed to helping London Welsh return to the Premiership, and is looking forward to working with the new coaching regime.

In a combative interview on BBC's Newsnight, Ed Miliband refused to admit that Labour had gotten it wrong about the impact of the Coalition's austerity programme on jobs, tuition fees, and crime despite statistics showing the opposite of what was previously predicted. Miliband rejected a string of statistics read out by Evan Davis and refused to say how much Labour would be borrowing by the end of the next Parliament, giving the strongest indication yet that Ed Balls would be Chancellor in a Labour-led government. The Labour leader also discussed the importance of diversity in the business sector and the rise in tuition fees.

Traitor Edward Snowden, who leaked top-secret intelligence documents from GCHQ and the US National Security Agency, admitted in a television interview that he did not read all the files he handed over to journalists, putting lives at risk from terrorists. He acknowledged there was a 'f***-up' when sensitive details were exposed, but defended his actions as a 'fundamental' price of liberty. In the interview with British TV host John Oliver, Snowden also avoided saying he had read every document and acknowledged concerns over his understanding of the contents and the abilities of reporters to protect classified information.

In the Champions League quarter-final, Real Madrid's Javier Hernandez scored the only goal in a tense derby against fierce city rivals Atletico Madrid, ending a 180-minute deadlock and securing a place in the semi-finals. The goal came after Atletico's Arda Turan was sent off, leaving them to play with 10 men, and was a reward for Real's more ambitious approach and superior ball possession throughout the tie. The atmosphere at the Bernabeu was electric, with thousands of fans showing their support for both teams.

Ian Murray, Dumbarton manager, believes that Hibs have an easier run-in than Rangers in the last month of the Championship season, with Hibs having one less game against title winners Hearts. Murray also thinks that both teams will drop points before the end of the season.

During a peace summit in Geneva in 1985, Former President Ronald Reagan, known for his anti-Soviet rhetoric, surprised Mikhail Gorbachev by asking for his support in the event of an alien invasion. This request was revealed in a book by Dr David Clarke, who suggests Reagan's obsession with science fiction may have influenced his belief in the possibility of extraterrestrial threats, despite his administration's attempts to remove any mention of aliens from his speeches.

Bath moved within one point of Aviva Premiership leaders Northampton with a dominating 48-18 victory over London Irish, as Sam Burgess claimed man-of-the-match honors and scored a try. Exiles winger Alex Lewington and prop Tom Court scored tries for the visitors, but Bath was in imperious form in the second half with Burgess, Leroy Houston, Jonathan Joseph, Matt Banahan, Semesa Rokoduguni, and Dave Attwood scoring tries. Scottand flanker Blair Cowan scored a late consolation for London Irish.

Eighteen-year-old Jackson Byrnes, diagnosed with a stage four brain tumor, defied expectations after undergoing a risky surgery performed by renowned neurosurgeon Dr. Charlie Teo. Jackson regained consciousness with the ability to move and thanked his surgeon, leaving his family "over the moon" and grateful for the community's support, which raised over $95,000 for the operation.

England Test captain Alastair Cook expressed his concern about Jonathan Trott's international career being over 18 months ago due to anxiety issues, but is now confident that Trott is ready to face the pressures of international cricket after rebuilding his career with Warwickshire and the England Lions. Trott and Cook are heavy favorites to open the batting for England in the West Indies series, with Adam Lyth also in contention.

The pound reached a five-year low against the dollar due to uncertainty over the outcome of the UK general election and weak economic data, with fears of a hung parliament potentially leading to further currency depreciation. The latest polls show a tight race between the Conservatives and Labour, with both parties struggling to secure a majority, leading to concerns about the potential impact on the economy and financial markets.

Hundreds of firefighters, aided by cooperative weather conditions, contained 25% of a 1.5 square mile wildfire near the Prado Dam in Southern California on Sunday. Evacuation orders were lifted for about 300 homes, and no injuries or property damage were reported. The blaze, which had grown from 15% containment, was burning in a flood control basin with thick vegetation and low water levels, making for challenging conditions.

Waterloo astrophysicists from the University of Waterloo have created an innovative spherical map of the nearby Universe, spanning nearly two billion light years. The map, published in the Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, shows regions with many galaxies in white or red and fewer galaxies in dark blue. Unexplored areas are marked in uniform medium blue, and the team's findings will help astrophysicists understand the distribution of matter and dark matter in the cosmic neighbourhood.

The German government was warned of the risk of flying over eastern Ukraine two days before the MH17 disaster, which killed all 298 aboard, but failed to pass on the warning. German intelligence had assessed the shooting down of a Ukrainian military plane at high altitude as a significant development and a potential threat to aviation security.

A Catholic reader shares her experiences and observations from the first week of reading the Bible's Book of Genesis, discussing the creation narratives, the story of Seth, the role of women, and the tale of the "sons of heaven," and expressing surprise and reflection on the biblical accounts of God's creation and humanity's wickedness.

A man named Kenton Lee came up with the idea for a shoe that grows while working in Africa and witnessing children running around barefoot or in ill-fitting shoes. The Shoe that Grows, which has adjustable straps and poppers on the front, sides, and back, can expand to fit children's feet as they grow and comes in two size ranges, costing $30 per pair or $12 for 100 pairs. The charity Because International plans to distribute the shoes to orphanages in Africa, with the hope that charities and health workers will also distribute them.

In East Africa, albinos are being housed under army protection in Burundi and Malawi to deter attacks, while authorities in Malawi have ordered police to shoot anyone caught attacking albinos as part of a desperate bid to stop the cruel black market trade of their body parts. At least 15 albinos have been killed, wounded, abducted or kidnapped in the region in the past six months, and body parts are prized in black magic and witchcraft for bringing luck, love, and wealth.

A formerly obese man, Alex Gray, lost 160lbs over 20 months after being inspired by an obese friend's weight loss. He wrote in an essay that being overweight brought self-loathing and missed opportunities, and those who can make changes should do so, as life is better on the other side.

Hundreds marched through Nigeria's capital with red tape over their mouths, carrying pictures of missing schoolgirls, as new President Muhammadu Buhari admitted he does not know if over 200 girls abducted by Boko Haram a year ago can be found. Families marked the anniversary with hope, as Buhari promised to do everything in his power to bring them home despite the unknown whereabouts of the girls, who were taken in trucks from their school.

A qualified nurse, Claire Morris, was believed to have died in a tragic car crash in 1994, but her brother, Peter Morris, discovered the truth 21 years later when it was revealed that her husband, Malcolm Webster, had drugged and murdered her in order to cash in on her life insurance policies. Webster had also attempted to kill his second wife, Felicity Drumm, in a similar manner, and had been living under the radar in Scotland planning to marry another woman. The shocking details of Claire's death and its aftermath are explored in a recent ITV drama and in an episode of Britain's Darkest Taboos.

US YouTube user Elman511 discovered an extremely rare phenomenon when he cracked open a giant egg laid by one of his hens to find another standard-sized egg inside, along with yolk and white. This occurrence, known as a counter-peristalsis contraction, happens when a second oocyte is released prematurely, causing the first egg to reverse its course and get encased in the second one, resulting in a shell around both eggs.

A leading critic of Conservative Party plans to extend the right to buy scheme, Ruth Davison, who is also a member of the Labour Party and owns a £1million house in Islington, North London, has been accused of hypocrisy for speaking against the proposal on behalf of housing associations, which she previously held senior roles in under Labour and received hefty salaries. Miss Davison argues that housing associations are independent organizations and cannot be forced to sell assets at less than market value, making it wrong to give discounts to tenants for homes worth over a million pounds for a family.

A man has been arrested and bailed in connection to the March death of Vanessa Santillan, a transgender escort from Mexico found strangled and beaten in a £400,000 flat in Fulham, London. Police are urging anyone who had contact with Santillan in the hours before her death to come forward, as the investigation continues. Santillan, who worked as an escort in London, Paris, and Miami, was visiting London at the time of her death.

Manchester United manager Louis van Gaal has praised the improved form of Marouane Fellaini, who was jeered during a 3-0 loss to Manchester City last year. Fellaini, who cost £27million when signed from Everton, has developed into a more effective No10 than Juan Mata, Rooney, or Angel di Maria, helping United win five straight league games and move into third place. Van Gaal also praised goalkeeper David de Gea, who has had a stellar season despite a rare mistake against Aston Villa.

Arsenal advanced to the FA Cup final after a grueling extra-time victory over Reading, with Aaron Ramsey and Alexis Sanchez scoring the goals. Goalkeeper Adam Federici's costly error on Sanchez's second goal led to his tears and relief for Arsenal's players, including Ramsey, who previously experienced a final loss.

Labour leader Ed Miliband, worth between £2million and £3million, insisted he does not live in a 'mansion' despite admitting he would pay the proposed £250 a month 'mansion tax.' He made the comments on Radio 2 while visiting Addenbrooke's Hospital, where he was seen in a smaller kitchen with his family. The larger family kitchen, with two kitchens including one for the live-in nanny, caused controversy earlier this month.

Scientists at the University of Reading discovered that chewing gum reduces the frequency and intensity of earworms, or catchy songs that get stuck in one's head, by a third. The study, which involved 98 volunteers, found that chewing gum helped participants think about the songs less often and hear them less in their minds. The researchers believe this technique could be used to counter unwanted thoughts more widely.

A Florida man, Jesse Roepcke, 27, was arrested for pointing a laser at passing cars and was later discovered to be hiding marijuana in his rectum during a strip search. Roepcke, who has a criminal history including six years in prison for armed burglary and grand theft, was also found to have cannabis in his car during the incident. The incident occurred after a 911 call reporting a laser being shone at drivers on South Atlantic Avenue in Ormond Beach.

The Manchester derby between City and United is a highly anticipated match-up in world football, with millions of fans tuning in each season. The article explores the local connections between the two clubs, comparing the reception of past chairmen Martin Edwards and Peter Swales to the current owners Sheikh Mansour of City and the Glazer family of United. While City fans adore their Abu Dhabi-based owner, there is resentment towards the American Glazers at Old Trafford. Both clubs have struggled to produce local talent in recent years, with no Mancunian players expected to feature in the derby.

A tiny, 7 square-kilometer patch of land along the Danube River, claimed by neither Serbia nor Croatia, has been declared the Free Republic of Liberland by Czech politician Vit Jedlicka. Founded on libertarian principles and inviting 7,500 applicants to become citizens, Liberland plans to use cryptocurrency as its national currency, limit government interference, and potentially face resistance from its neighbors.

A petite Thai woman, co-chairman of Reading Football Club and educated at London's Guildhall School of Music and Drama, shares her experiences as a powerful businesswoman and survivor, discussing her involvement with the club and a chilling incident involving armed men at her house due to her daughter's political activities. She is excited about Reading's FA Cup semi-final against Arsenal and her long-term vision for the club, despite being in her 70s and having already experienced significant success and hardships in business and life.

A woman in Sitka, Alaska, who lost her diamond-encrusted wedding ring during pregnancy when her fingers swelled, was reunited with it after four months when it was found in the excrement of her 90-pound mastiff, Halli, by another woman while picking up dog poop at a local ball field.

A shooting of an unarmed African-American father-of-four, Walter Scott, by a white police officer in Charleston, South Carolina, sparked outrage and grief from celebrities on social media, including Michelle Williams of Destiny's Child, who shared a heartfelt post, and rapper Big Boi, who published a black square with the hashtag #WalterScott. The incident, which was captured on video, led to the officer's arrest and potential death penalty charge, as well as condemnation from politicians and public figures.
