The secluded North Sentinel Island in the Indian Ocean, known for its picturesque beaches and lush forest, is avoided by tourists and fishermen due to the violent reactions of its indigenous Sentinelese inhabitants, who have killed two men in the past and have attacked planes and helicopters. The tribe, believed to have lived there for 60,000 years, rejects modern civilization and prefers to maintain zero contact with the outside world. The Indian government has established a three-mile exclusion zone around the island to protect the Sentinelese people and preserve their way of life, which is under threat from illegal fishing and outside intrusions.

A 32-year-old man has been charged with the murder of an 80-year-old British woman, Violet Price, whose body was found in two different areas of a southern French region. Police had been searching for Price since she went missing from her home in Moustier village after attending a dinner party. The suspect, who is already serving time for manslaughter, had told officers where to find the body parts.

A new primate species, Macaca leucogenys, also known as the white-cheeked macaque, was discovered in the remote highlands of southeast Tibet through the observation of its distinctive rounded penis and other unique features, such as pale whiskers, long thick neck hairs, and a high-pitched alarm call. The discovery highlights the importance of biodiversity conservation in the region, as the species may be under threat from illegal hunting and hydropower construction.

One Direction star Niall Horan led tributes to BBC broadcaster Colin Bloomfield, who died from skin cancer at the age of 33. Bloomfield, a popular figure at BBC Radio Derby for 10 years as a presenter, reporter, and Derby County commentator, passed away at a hospice near his hometown of Shrewsbury. Fellow broadcasters and Derby County supporters paid tribute to the 'consummate professional' and 'perfect colleague.'

A headmaster at Sherborne Preparatory School in Dorset has urged parents to trust schools and teachers, rather than interfering in their children's education and becoming "dervishes" ready to battle on their behalf. Mr. Tait, writing in the Independent Association of Prep Schools magazine Attain, claimed that modern parents are bombarded with information and need to use their intuition to trust the educational process. Meanwhile, a survey by the NASUWT teachers' union revealed an increase in parents using social media to intimidate staff with obscenities, threats, and covert films.

Militants fighting for the Islamic State in Syria and Iraq are led by a secretive group of strategists and officials who were once senior figures in Saddam Hussein's army, making up almost all of the regional commanders and overseeing ISIS' day-to-day running. Their military expertise and Baathist army connections, particularly in oil smuggling, are crucial to ISIS' income.

A parolee, John McCallum, was sentenced to at least 20 years in jail for crashing into and killing a newlywed mother, Francesca Weatherhead, 25, at an intersection in Detroit while fleeing police following a botched burglary. Her husband, Matthew, expressed his loss and shared how she was a strength to their family and would have been a great mother. Francesca's younger sister, Nina Vitale, also expressed her grief. The incident caused outrage as McCallum had been paroled just months prior despite being eligible since 2013 and having a maximum sentence of 20 years on his latest arrest. McCallum, 27, ran a red light in his Ford Escape and struck Francesca's car, who had graduated from Trenton High School and Michigan State University, where she met her husband and was putting him through medical school.

Australians have significantly overspent on digital devices and services in the last year, with an average expenditure of $2,300 per person, according to a report from Suncorp Bank. Men spent more than women, and families with children at home spent 50% more than other household types. Mobile phone bills were the biggest expense and caused the greatest disparity for families. Over one third of households don't budget for technology and underestimate their spending.

A woman from Milton Keynes whose son was born severely premature after she was diagnosed with bacterial vaginosis (BV) has urged others to be aware of the infection, which affects one in three women and can lead to miscarriage and premature birth. Callum Butler was born at 25 weeks and underwent heart surgery, laser eye surgery, and 10 blood transfusions. His mother, Amanda Butler, discovered she had the condition after her premature labour, which went undiagnosed for months despite symptoms including discharge and a strong smell. BV is caused by an imbalance of internal bacteria and can increase the risk of contracting STIs. It is twice as common as thrush and can be treated with antibiotics.

In an article on CNN, it is discussed how the Democratic Party's reliance on campaign donations from Wall Street and big businesses is limiting their ability to address economic inequality and take on financial institutions, as evidenced by the upcoming leadership change in the Senate and the role of super PAC donations in scandals. The article argues that campaign finance reform is necessary to allow for a more populist economic agenda and level the playing field.

A man, William Ziegler, who spent 15 years on death row in Alabama for a 2001 capital murder conviction, was freed after his conviction was quashed due to substandard legal representation, recanted testimony, and a juror's lies. Judge Sarah Stewart allowed him to plead guilty to aiding and abetting murder, sentencing him to the time already served. Ziegler's mother, O'Della Wilson, expressed anger over her son's guilty plea, while Baker's relatives were disappointed with his release.

Kim Sei-young holds a three-shot lead going into the final round of the ANA Inspiration after carding a three-under-par 69, with two bogeys halting her progress but successive birdies on the 16th and 17th moving her to 10 under. Lydia Ko, the world No 1, struggled with a second consecutive over-par score of 74, equalling Annika Sorenstam's LPGA Tour record of 29 consecutive under-par rounds in the process. American Stacy Lewis is second, one shot behind Thailand's Ariya Jutanugarn who shot a best-of-the-day 66.

Celtic manager Ronny Deila has confirmed the Scottish champions are considering a £400,000 offer for Hearts captain Danny Wilson, with the defender's contract expiring next summer. Despite Hearts owner Ann Budge insisting they have no need to sell, Celtic are looking for central defensive reinforcements due to the uncertain futures of Virgil van Dijk and Jason Denayer.

The Duchess of Cambridge, with Prince George, visited Snettisham Farm Park in Norfolk before the birth of their new baby, enjoying a typical day out as they continue to create a secluded, middle-class life for their family at Anmer Hall. William and Kate will be making Anmer their principal residence, with Kate taking a longer maternity leave and William scaling back his charity commitments to focus on family and flying. Staff at the farm know the royal family well and describe them as "absolutely lovely" and "delightful."

Hibernian claimed a 2-0 victory over Hearts in the Edinburgh derby, with Jason Cummings and Farid El Alagui scoring the goals. Hibs dominated the first half and were rewarded for their efforts with Cummings' close-range finish, while El Alagui sealed the win in injury time. Alan Stubbs' side celebrated their first derby win of the season, with Neil Alexander making several saves for Hearts. Martin Boyle and Liam Craig were among those celebrating with El Alagui following the victory.

The article expresses concerns that U.S. President Barack Obama's recent statements on Middle Eastern policy, known as the "Obama doctrine," do not align with the current reality on the ground. The article criticizes the U.S. for focusing solely on terrorism and nuclear proliferation while ignoring other core interests, such as maintaining peace and order, and for failing to address Iran's human rights abuses and regional interference. The Arab world is skeptical of U.S. assurances and is taking matters into its own hands, as seen in the ongoing military intervention in Yemen.

A survey of 1,000 adults by YouNeedaBudget.com found that 64% of people confess to spending more money when shopping with friends, blaming peer pressure and the urge to show off. Despite this, 85% give themselves a budget when shopping, but 95% still end up overspending on items such as food, clothing, and entertainment. A spokesperson for the company advised setting priorities and sticking to long-term goals to avoid impulse purchases. Some justifiers for overspending included sales, special treats, and necessity. Nearly one in five respondents hid big purchases from their significant others.

A heavily armed 24-year-old Algerian jihadi suspected of planning to attack churches in Paris was arrested hours after the murder of a young woman in the Paris suburb of Villejuif. Police found Arabic documents mentioning ISIS and Al Qaeda at his home, as well as evidence of contact with a man in Syria requesting a church attack. The suspect, named locally as Sid Ahmed Ghlam, had reportedly been living in a student residence and was officially under surveillance due to his ties to terrorism.

Nineteen-year-old basketball player Lauren Hill, who is battling a rare and aggressive brain tumor with a survival rate of less than 10 percent, spent her final days raising over $1.5 million for cancer research and made it through a full season at Mount St. Joseph's University while expressing her desire to help others. She continues to fight her disease and aims to raise a total of $2.2 million for treatment and research. A video of her journey and progress can be found in the article.

A Louisville, Kentucky man's sad faces in his vacation photos to Puerto Rico have gone viral after he won the trip through his workplace but couldn't bring his wife to care for their six-month-old daughter. The photos, which show him looking miserable while snorkeling, playing slot machines, and sipping drinks, have amused millions online, despite the man stating that he had a good time on the work trip.

Andy Murray reached the Miami Open final with a clinical 6-4, 6-4 victory over Tomas Berdych, setting up a showdown with Novak Djokovic. The Scot, who is set to overtake Rafael Nadal in the rankings, dispatched John Isner in the semifinals and produced an excellent performance against Berdych, who was accompanied by Murray's former coach Dani Vallverdu and trainer Jez Green.

A talking dog named Miss Wendy on Britain's Got Talent stunned the judges with her singing skills and humor, but some viewers accused the act of being cruel due to the use of a fake jaw controlled by a remote. The RSPCA and Ofcom received numerous complaints, and the organization stated they would be contacting the show to inquire about the methods used. A similar illusion was revealed to have been used on America's Got Talent in 2012. Another act, a free-runner named Matt McCreary, also impressed the judges with a daring performance.

John Tuite, 22, and Carlos Santolalla, 25, also known as Jarlos, are the first openly gay couple to be signed together to a modeling agency, Fusion Models. They had previously faced pressure from their old agencies to act straight and delete their joint Instagram account where they often post loving images of themselves. Despite facing obstacles, they have built a following in the industry and on the New York City party scene, working with big brands like Edun.

Over 1,500 migrants were rescued by the Italian navy and coast guard off the Libyan coast in five separate operations, as reported by officials on Sunday. Three boats in distress sent rescue requests, leading the coast guard to discover two more in need, bringing the total to five. Human traffickers organized the dangerous sea crossings, and about 170,000 migrants entered the European Union through Italy last year, resulting in over 3,000 fatalities. Arrivals into Italy via the European Union were up 43% in the first two months of this year compared to the same period in 2014.

A new video reveals significant price differences between identical products and services marketed towards women and men, dubbed the 'women's tax.' The video highlights disparities in pricing for items such as razors, perfume, and dry-cleaning, with women often paying more for the same product. Despite some improvements, the report notes that women in the US continue to pay approximately $1,351 more annually due to gender-based pricing, and the practice remains legal in most states.

A 25-year-old Hungarian man, Andras Janos Vass, has been found guilty of human trafficking and racketeering in Miami for luring young, gay Eastern European men to the US with promises of work, then keeping them captive and forcing them into sex slavery, spending up to 20 hours a day on sex work. Two men testified that they were lured via the Planet Romeo dating site and faced threats to their families if they refused. Vass faces a maximum sentence of 155 years in prison.

A Japanese artist, Megumi Igarashi, was charged with obscenity for distributing plans to build a kayak modeled after her vagina. She denies the charges and argues that her work is art, despite facing up to two years in jail and thousands signing a petition for her release.

A married mother of two with multiple myeloma was ejected from an Alaska Airlines flight due to her lack of a doctor's note clearing her to fly while wearing a surgical mask to shield herself from germs. The incident, which was recorded and viewed thousands of times on social media, caused outrage and resulted in the airline refunding the family's tickets and donating the value to multiple myeloma research.

Morrisons has been named the best place for breakfast in the UK by the Menu Innovation and Development Awards (MIDAS), beating restaurants, pubs, and hotels, with its extensive menu offering a variety of options and excellent value for money. The supermarket chain was praised for its food quality and customer satisfaction.

In the horror of the Bergen-Belsen concentration camp, liberated by the British Army 70 years ago, a British soldier, Sergeant Norman Turgel, fell in love with a teenage Polish inmate, Gena, thanks to the kindness of their commander, Major Leonard Berney, who facilitated their marriage and ensured they stayed connected during the chaotic post-liberation period. The camp, which held over 60,000 starved and ill prisoners, was in the grip of a typhus outbreak with 500 dying daily, and was later burned to the ground to destroy the suffering.

UNICEF delivered 16 tons of medical supplies to Yemen's capital, Sanaa, to help 80,000 people, but the situation remains dire as access to water, sanitation, and health services worsens. Meanwhile, about 900 refugees from Yemen have arrived in the Horn of Africa, and the UN is concerned about the safety of those attempting the dangerous sea crossing due to fuel shortages and increased boat traffic. At least 643 people have been killed and 2,226 injured in the conflict since March 19.

During a visit to a primary school near Bolton to unveil the Tory's new education policy, Prime Minister David Cameron was upstaged by six-year-old Lucy Howarth who pulled funny faces and struggled with a tricky passage from 'Prince Ronald.' The policy proposes that children who achieve poor SATs results will be forced to resit them in secondary school, affecting 100,000 pupils. Despite the distraction, Cameron shared a joke with Lucy, who showed no signs of being fazed. The proposal received criticism from Labour and Lib Dems, with concerns over the potential bureaucracy and impact on resources.

A New Zealand family of fourteen who recently left the secluded Gloriavale Christian Community, which shuns the outside world and rejects birth control, is adjusting well to modern life in Timaru with the father securing a farm job and the family moving into a home with modern appliances. Former residents have described the challenges of adapting to the outside world, including dealing with financial matters and accessing personal technology.

Manchester City's sporting director Txiki Begiristain scouted Porto players Yacine Brahimi and Alex Sandro during their Champions League match against Bayern Munich, with both Brazilian international Sandro and Algerian winger Brahimi on City's summer transfer list, along with Wolfsburg midfielder Kevin de Bruyne. City are looking to strengthen their squad as their Premier League title defense is faltering, with manager Manuel Pellegrini under pressure to deliver results.

Borussia Dortmund have announced Thomas Tuchel as the successor to Jurgen Klopp, who is leaving the club after seven years. Tuchel, who previously managed Mainz and is known for his attacking style, will take over a three-year deal starting in July. He has expressed admiration for Pep Guardiola and has been influenced by Hermann Badstuber, a youth coach at Stuttgart and father of Holger Badstuber.

In this article, CNN interviews survivors and victims' families of the 2012 Aurora, Colorado "Dark Knight Rises" theater shooting, as the accused gunman, James Holmes, goes on trial for 165 counts including murder and attempted murder. The interviewees, including Pierce O'Farrill, Kaylan Bailey, Tom and Caren Teves, and Marcus Weaver, share their experiences and feelings about the trial and the lasting impact the shooting has had on their lives. O'Farrill, who was shot multiple times but survived, reflects on the strength he's gained from the experience, while Bailey, who made a heartrending 911 call during the shooting, discusses the life-changing impact it has had. The Teveses, who lost their son Alex, and Weaver, who lost his friend Rebecca, share their memories of the victims and their thoughts on forgiveness and justice.

April 27, known as Freedom Day in South Africa, was celebrated this year with mixed feelings as the country commemorated its first democratic elections in 1994 and the end of apartheid, while also dealing with a recent spate of anti-immigrant violence that claimed lives. People and governments, including Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, took to Twitter to celebrate, send out wishes, and denounce the violence, with many also remembering Nelson Mandela. Google honored the day with a special doodle.

Internationally recognized designers Becky Cooper and Bridget Yorston, also known as Bec and Bridge, showcased their new swimwear line at Mercedes-Benz Fashion Week Australia, featuring animal prints, metallics, and cut-out styles. The collection was inspired by Seventies model Veruschka and the 1970s in Marrakech, with models wearing flowing maxi skirts, dark jewel-toned dresses, and metallic swimsuits, as well as accessories like gold jewelry, Gladiator sandals, and beaded crop tops. The show included metallic fringe dresses, fishnet overswim, and even a silver metallic "tin-man" suit. The metallic swimwear stole the show, with low-cut sport luxe waistbands and gold arm cuffs and oversized earrings. The collection was completed with leather laced open-toe heels and bronzed skin courtesy of St Tropez. Attendees included Jecinta Campbell, Rebecca Judd, Carissa Walford, and Jesinta Campbell, who wore leather laced heels similar to the models.

Mum-of-three Natalie Prescott, 26, nearly lost her legs after diving 40 feet off Appley Bridge quarry in Wigan in 2012. Through perseverance and the inspiration of her children, she is finally making a recovery. However, after a teenage boy drowned in the same quarry, Natalie is campaigning to have the area closed off to the public.

Tottenham Hotspur chairman Daniel Levy is reportedly planning a new transfer policy focused on buying young players for £10-15 million with potential for resale, but this short-term approach contrasts with elite clubs' strategies and may hinder Tottenham's progress, as seen in past sales of top performers like Luka Modric, Dimitar Berbatov, and Michael Carrick.

Ten top doctors have urged Columbia University to remove Mehmet Oz, vice chairman and professor of surgery at its College of Physicians and Surgeons, from his faculty position due to concerns over his promotion of questionable treatments and conflicts of interest, despite the school's commitment to academic freedom.

The Atlantis, The Palm resort in Dubai offers two underwater hotel rooms, Poseidon and Neptune, with floor-to-ceiling views into the Ambassador Lagoon aquarium, featuring 65,000 marine animals, creating a unique under-the-sea experience. These three-storey suites, with a base price of £5,450 ($8,200) a night, include perks such as 24-carat gold soap and private butler service, making guests feel pampered and immersed in luxury.

A 20-30 foot long fragment of a fiberglass boat suspected to have originated from the 2011 Japanese earthquake and tsunami was towed to Newport, Oregon. Inside were found yellowtail jack fish, a variety normally found in Japanese waters. The remnants will be inspected further and taken to a landfill, while the fish will be taken to the Oregon Coast Aquarium.

A noose was discovered hanging from a tree at Duke University outside a building that houses several offices focused on diversity, including the Center for Sexual and Gender Diversity and the Center for Multicultural Affairs, in Durham, North Carolina. Duke University officials and police are investigating the incident, and anyone found responsible will be held accountable. Students, especially students of color, expressed fear and disappointment on social media, with some believing it was racially motivated. The noose was removed from the tree shortly after it was discovered. The incident comes just two weeks after students allegedly chanted a racist song at a black female student. Duke University leaders have scheduled a forum to address racial equality on campus.

The Islamic State's self-declared capital Raqqa is facing food shortages and long lines for assistance, as documented by the anti-ISIS group Raqqa is Being Slaughtered Silently. The images contradict the prosperous image ISIS tries to project and highlight the hardships faced by civilians under their rule, including hours of power outages and food scarcity.
A 16-year-old boy, who famously asked a church for help in finding a forever home in 2013, has finally been adopted by his social worker after several failed attempts and multiple foster homes.

Two men have been arrested for allegedly gang raping an unconscious woman on a Florida beach during Spring Break, as hundreds of people watched without intervening. The video was discovered during an unrelated investigation in Alabama, leading to the identification and arrest of Delone' Martistee, 22, and Ryan Austin Calhoun, 23. The woman, a student at Troy University, believes she was drugged before the attack.

A new interactive map reveals significant variation in public concern about global warming across the United States, with concern ranging from a low of 38% in Pickett County, Tennessee, to a high of 74% in Washington, D.C. The map, based on data from Yale University, also shows that concern about global warming varies widely within states and between different demographic groups. Nationally, 52% of Americans are worried about global warming, but this figure masks the geographic diversity of opinion.

Manny Pacquiao trained outside at Griffith Park in Los Angeles, running with fitness fanatics and practicing shadow boxing, while also showcasing his new multicolored mouthguard ahead of his upcoming fight against Floyd Mayweather.

An enormous European eagle owl in the Dutch town of Noordeinde has been terrorizing residents for months, targeting them by perching on their heads. The wild bird, which has a wingspan of up to 75cm and weighs around 6lbs, has become a local attraction, drawing twitchers from across the country who have heard of its unusual behavior. The owl's latest attack was caught on video as it perched on a woman's head for about a minute before flying off.

The Champions of the English Aviva Premiership, Northampton Saints, suffered a brutal defeat against French Top 14 powerhouse Clermont Auvergne in the Champions Cup, with Noa Nakaitaci scoring two tries and Nick Abendanon adding one in a masterful display. The English side, leading the league but imperious in the French arena, were obliterated, losing confidence and key players Courtney Lawes and Alex Corbisiero to injury.

In eastern Afghanistan, a suicide bomber killed 17 people and injured 64, including Afghan parliament member Humayoon Humayoon, as they were on their way to join a larger protest against the provincial governor in Khost city. Taliban denied responsibility, and no other organization has claimed the attack. CNN reported the incident, with Masoud Popalzai in Kabul, Elizabeth Joseph contributing, and Jethro Mullen writing from Hong Kong.

A Chinese court convicted veteran journalist Gao Yu to seven years in prison for revealing state secrets, despite her lawyer's claim that her confession was extracted under duress and retracted. The authorities accused her of leaking a confidential Communist Party document to an overseas news organization, and Amnesty International criticized the ruling as part of a crackdown on dissent and information control under President Xi Jinping.

An inventor, Eason Chow, has created dissolvable coffee capsules inspired by sugar-coated sweets in gumball machines to address the issue of waste generated by home coffee machines. The sugar-coated pods, called Droops, consist of coffee granules covered in milk powder and sugar, and will be available in varying thicknesses and flavors.

Reality TV stars Kim Kardashian, Kanye West, and their family have traveled to Armenia for an eight-day visit to explore the mountainous landscapes and rich history of their late father's birthplace. The celebrities, accompanied by a large entourage, have expressed excitement about discovering their roots and experiencing the culturally and economically vibrant capital city of Yerevan.

Bayern Munich doctor Hans-Wilhelm Muller-Wohlfahrt, 72, and his team, including Lutz Hansel, have resigned from the German champions following the blame placed on the medical department for their shock Champions League defeat by Porto. Muller-Wohlfahrt, renowned for his unconventional methods using substances like goat's blood and hen's comb, has served Bayern for almost 40 years and has treated numerous athletes, including Usain Bolt. No payment was required for his treatment when the Liverpool player, Jamie Redknapp, visited him for a knee injury.

Yorkshire have signed India international Cheteshwar Pujara as their overseas player for the LV= County Championship, replacing Younis Khan who is now looking to join Pakistan's squad for their tour of Bangladesh. Pujara, a highly-rated Test batsman with six centuries to his name, joins Yorkshire until May and is eager to make his mark in English cricket.

The article reveals that some NHS executives have received large pay rises and bonuses, including through pension loopholes and compensation payments, sparking outrage from political parties, healthcare professionals, and patient groups. A future Conservative government has pledged to investigate the findings in detail, while Professor Sir Brian Jarman called for an independent review to ensure NHS CEO salaries are fair and related to the size of the organization they run.

A mother and her former partner, Kirsty Lee and Alistair Wayne Bowen, appeared in court over the death of Lee's two-year-old daughter, Cara Lee-Fanus, who suffered fatal head injuries and extensive bruises and burn marks in Chertsey, Surrey. The pair were charged with causing or allowing the death of a child and causing or allowing serious physical harm to a child and were granted bail, with their case set to be heard at Guildford Crown Court on May 9.

The article discusses Tony Blackburn's call for lessons to be learned from the historic sex abuse scandals that tarnished the BBC, specifically the cases of Jimmy Savile, Rolf Harris, and Paul Gambaccini. Blackburn expresses outrage over the way his friend and colleague Gambaccini was treated despite the charges against him being dropped. He also reflects on his own controversies and past indiscretions.

An Armenian expat, Yervand Garginian, who moved to Australia from Armenia in 1977, became an internet sensation after his daughter Elizabeth posted a video of him dismissing the Australian-style barbecue and demonstrating the traditional 'khorovats' recipe at his nephew Albert's house. The video, which has been viewed over five million times, shows Mr. Garginian cooking chicken on an open charcoal barbecue and sharing his family's secret recipe.

Saudi Arabia resumed airstrikes on Yemen's southern regions on Wednesday despite Houthi rebels calling for peace talks, following the end of Operation Decisive Storm. The UN-sponsored peace talks had failed earlier in January due to rebel attacks, and the new initiative, Operation Renewal of Hope, focuses on the political process. However, rebel attacks on government forces prompted the resumption of airstrikes, targeting a weapons depot in Taiz province. Saudi Arabia aims to restore the Yemeni government and protect civilians, while also confronting terrorism and creating an international coalition for maritime security.

Chicago Mayor Rahm Emanuel secured a hard-fought victory in the run-off election against Jesus 'Chuy' Garcia, thanks to intensive campaigning and support from his powerful brother Ari Emanuel and former boss, President Obama. Despite criticisms of his brusque management style and financial mismanagement, Emanuel won about 56% of the votes, while Garcia conceded with around 44%. The election saw a record number of early voters and marked the first runoff race since Chicago switched to non-partisan elections 20 years ago. Emanuel faces significant challenges in his second term, including fixing underfunded pension systems, addressing ongoing concerns about crime, and dealing with labor unions.

Manchester United have offered goalkeeper David de Gea a record-breaking £200,000-a-week contract to prevent him from joining Real Madrid as a free agent next summer, but there is no breakthrough in negotiations yet. United have also signed former Barcelona keeper Victor Valdes as a backup plan.

Irish betting company Paddy Power has apologized for a controversial tweet comparing Newcastle United's losses to Liverpool to the number of unarmed African-American men who have been killed by police in the US. The tweet, which was a pun on the name of Liverpool's famous home end, has been deleted and sparked outrage on social media. The tweet came ahead of their English Premier League match on Monday.

Ed Miliband promised to establish a Home Office task force to increase prosecutions and fines against employers exploiting migrant workers, stating that it drives low-skilled migration and holds down wages for British workers. Nigel Farage and Tory Immigration Minister James Brokenshire dismissed Miliband's ideas, with Farage asserting that controlling immigration was the main issue. Miliband also pledged to secure the borders with 1,000 extra staff at ports and help integration, including requiring English language skills for certain jobs.

Choupette, a three-year-old Birman cat and the pride of fashion designer Karl Lagerfeld, has become a modeling sensation with over £2 million in fees last year, surpassing Cara Delevingne. With a first-class pedigree and a team of six, Choupette refuses to promote cheap brands and lives a luxurious lifestyle, including bespoke mineral water, silver dishes, and fine dining at Parisian restaurants. Lagerfeld, who calls himself "Daddy," has taken Choupette from a borrowed cat to an international star, despite her being stolen from her original owner, French model Baptiste Giabiconi. Choupette's fame has earned her a Twitter and Instagram following, Wikipedia entry, and even a £1,660 Louis Vuitton pet carrier.

A senior police officer has issued a warning that ISIS is building a network of "misfits" around the world, including vulnerable individuals, young people, and those with mental health issues, to carry out chaotic terror attacks with little planning. The tactics of terror groups are changing, with an increase in attacks using knives and vehicles, and ISIS is exploiting social media to radicalize and inspire individuals to commit terror acts.

A man identified as Terry Martin, 48, fatally shot his longtime girlfriend, Laurice Hampton, 48, after she requested half of his $500 lottery winnings. Martin, who had a history of domestic violence against Hampton, died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound to the head shortly after the shooting, which occurred in their master bedroom in Fort Worth, Texas.

An angry Darren Humphries, 37, was sentenced to eight weeks in jail for assaulting his estranged wife, Claire, 31, by grabbing her and throwing chocolate mini eggs at her during an argument over access to their children. In a separate incident, he also damaged her car by throwing wheelie bins.

Former world champion Ken Doherty defeated Reanne Evans in a thrilling and intense match during the snooker championship qualifiers, preventing her from becoming the first woman to play at Sheffield's Crucible Theatre. Despite the loss, Evans, a single mother and 10-time ladies' champion, gained significant attention and advanced the cause of women in sport. Critics and bookies who doubted her chances were proved wrong as Evans put Doherty under pressure throughout the match.

A politics teacher at the prestigious St. George's School in Ascot, attended by Sir Winston Churchill and Princess Beatrice, has been banned from teaching for at least five years following a hearing by the National Council for Teaching and Leadership after being caught on video exposing himself to a female passenger on a train in 2014. Nuku Vanonyi Cudjoe-Calvocoressi, 41, was convicted of indecent exposure and sentenced to community service and a fine, but the incident led to his removal from the classroom.

Christopher Eccleston, who grew up in Manchester and played Doctor Who with a strong northern accent, suggested he left the show in 2005 after disagreements with bosses over the accent. Eccleston, a working-class actor, criticized the lack of diversity in the industry and the dominance of privately-educated actors, leading to a milky, anodyne culture.

Arsenal's fans are divided over giving "respect" to Cesc Fabregas when he returns to the Emirates Stadium with Chelsea on Sunday, as Arsene Wenger hopes for a good reception for the former player he counts as deserving. However, some fans have called for the removal of a Fabregas banner and feel he does not deserve it due to the unclear circumstances surrounding his departure from Arsenal in 2011.

AP McCoy, who announced his intention to retire after riding Mr Mole to victory at Newbury in February, is one of three 7-2 co-favorites in the Grade One Celebration Chase at Sandown on Saturday, alongside Irish raider Special Tiara and Nicky Henderson's Sprinter Sacre. Mr Mole, trained by Paul Nicholls, will be ridden by Nico de Boinville in the absence of injured Barry Geraghty, and will be McCoy's last ride in a Grade One race. Six rivals, including Mick Channon's Somersby, will challenge the three favorites.

CNN reports that Britons are preparing to vote in a tightly contested election that could result in a change of power from the Conservative Party to Labour, or a coalition government. The article discusses the indirect nature of UK elections, the role of the Queen, and the key issues, including the NHS, immigration, the economy, and Europe. It also touches on political apathy and the impact of fringe parties, specifically UKIP, on the election.

The SFA judicial panel dismissed the charge against Inverness defender Josh Meekings for deliberate handball in the Scottish Cup semi-final against Celtic, ruling they were not entitled to apply retrospective punishment since the incident was seen by officials but no penalty was given. The decision raises questions about the SFA's prosecutor citing players for incidents not punished on the field.

A large gas pipeline operated by Pacific Gas & Electric Company exploded into a tower of fire on Friday in Central California, closing Highway 99 and injuring at least 15 people, including four critically, during roadwork being conducted by a county public works employee and inmates. The cause of the explosion is under investigation.

An eight-year-old girl identified as Piper died and her nine-year-old brother continues to fight for his life after their car aquaplaned and collided with a utility on Wyee Road in New South Wales' Central Coast on Saturday. The crash, which also left several others with injuries, resulted in the national Easter long weekend death toll reaching 10. A video of the scene is available on 9News.

Experts at Yale University revealed that an experimental cancer drug, AZDO5030, which was abandoned by Astra Zeneca due to poor cancer treatment results, has shown promise in reversing the effects of Alzheimer's disease in mice by blocking a process that damages nerve connections in the brain. Human trials are now underway to test the drug's efficacy in Alzheimer's patients.

LeBron James paid tribute to Lauren Hill, a 19-year-old basketball player who captured the nation's hearts as she battled inoperable brain cancer and raised $1.5 million, with an emotional letter praising her strength, courage, and leadership. Despite never meeting her, James was inspired by her determination and the impact she had on everyone around her. Hill, who was diagnosed with a rare form of brain cancer at 18 and defied doctors' expectations, passed away on Friday. The letter, which has been widely shared on social media, expressed James' admiration for Hill and expressed his condolences to her family. Despite the tragic news, the awareness and support for Hill's cause continued to grow, with many expressing their sadness and appreciation for her impact on their lives. Unlike other childhood cancers, there has been little progress in improving treatments and cure rates for Diffuse Intrinsic Pontine Glioma (DIPG), a condition that affects fewer than 10% of children who survive two years after diagnosis.

A five-year-old girl from Geelong, Victoria, named Ava, had to undergo complex skull surgery to correct a rare condition where two plates at the front of her skull had fused together prematurely, restricting the growth of her brain. Doctors discovered the condition during a routine check-up and removed her entire skull, smashing it into pieces and putting it back together like a jigsaw puzzle. Ava, now six, has made a full recovery and is a "completely different child" with more energy and enthusiasm for life.

A desperate mother is pleading for help after her 15-year-old autistic daughter, Kimberly Greenberg, went missing over a week ago while out for a walk near their Santa Monica home. Last seen wearing jeans, a red sweater, and Ugg boots, Kimberly left without her medication or cellphone. The LAPD is asking for information from the public, and those who wish to remain anonymous can contact Crime Stoppers.

In the transitional period between seasons, when the weather is unpredictable and new fashion trends emerge, UK stylist and fashion blogger Lily Melrose offers 15 budget-friendly DIY hacks to update last year's wardrobe for the current season using common household items and a glue gun. From creating a jeweled collar for a little black dress to embellishing sunglasses, these creative tips allow readers to embrace summer style without breaking the bank.

Many people dream of winning the lottery, but for 26-year-old Lisa Courtney from Hertfordshire, her dream is to work at the Pokemon Centre in Japan. Lisa holds the Guinness World Record for the largest collection of Pokemon memorabilia, with over 25,000 items filling her home, including toys, models, clothing, and posters. Her mother, Sharon, has even sacrificed her bedroom to make room for her daughter's extensive collection. Lisa's love for Pokemon began at age nine and helped her through difficult times, including bullying at school. Despite the sacrifice of space, her family is supportive of her obsession.

Football Shirt Friday took place in April, with celebrities and fans showing support for The Bobby Moore Fund for Cancer Research UK by wearing their favorite football shirts on social media. The annual event, which coincides with Bowel Cancer Awareness Month, encourages people to donate and raise awareness to fight against bowel cancer.

Lifetime is producing "The Unauthorized Full House Story," a follow-up to its "Unauthorized Saved By the Bell" movie. The film will explore the cast's rise to fame and the pressure they faced balancing on-screen and real-life challenges during the run of the beloved family sitcom. Casting is underway, with Ron McGee writing and Front Street Pictures and Ringaling Productions producing. No air date has been set.

In a narrow Sky Bet Championship defeat, Rotherham boss Steve Evans was left fuming after his side were denied a penalty when Rob Kiernan handled the ball in the box, leaving Tesche's first-half goals for Birmingham City standing. Cotterill and Donaldson scored for the hosts, while Derbyshire pulled one back for Rotherham, but Evans' side could not find an equalizer despite numerous chances.

A 22-year-old passenger on an Atlanta-bound Air Canada flight from Toronto was choked by a stranger sitting behind him, but was saved by teammates and other passengers. The suspect, who reportedly believed the soccer team was trying to kill him, was escorted off the plane for medical/mental evaluation after landing in Atlanta.

A former commander of the Metropolitan Police's elite Flying Squad, John O'Connor, believes the Hatton Garden gem heist and the massive underground fire in Holborn that occurred days earlier could be linked, suggesting the power outage was deliberate and the gang had inside help to execute the complex crime.

CNN reports that the upcoming release of a new, original Dr. Seuss book, "What Pet Should I Get?", has generated significant excitement, leading to a million-copy first printing and a wave of nostalgia on social media. The manuscript and illustrations were discovered in the late 1990s and will be the first new, original Dr. Seuss book since "Oh, the Places You'll Go!" in 1990.

CNN reports that according to a Wall Street Journal article, Chinese nuclear experts estimate North Korea may have up to 20 nuclear warheads, but the bigger concern is North Korea's potential to double its arsenal by the end of the year using uranium, making it a potential runaway nuclear weapons program with undetectable uranium-based facilities. Additionally, Adm. Bill Gortney's assessment that North Korea's KN-08 ICBM is operational with a warhead capacity raises concerns about Pyongyang's ability to miniaturize and launch nuclear weapons towards the US mainland, potentially undermining deterrence and increasing tensions in the region.

Tottenham Hotspur are making progress on their new £400million stadium development with the demolition of Archway Sheet Metal Works, the final obstacle to the move. The club plans to open a 'world-class' 56,000-seater stadium in three years time and have secured the necessary planning permission, despite a legal challenge.

Tennis star Eugenie Bouchard, 21, took a break from her early exits at the Miami Open to chill out with fellow Canadian and Olympic medalist Kerri Walsh Jennings and Sports Illustrated model Hannah Davis. Meanwhile, Serena Williams continued her unbeaten run in 2015, reaching the final with a 6-3, 6-4 victory over Carla Suarez Navarro.

An international panel of scientists, led by the Global Warming Policy Foundation, will launch an inquiry to examine whether adjustments and extrapolations made to world temperature records have exaggerated the extent of global warming, focusing on potential bias and the effect on thousands of stations whose data is included in the Met Office, Nasa, and US government agency records.

Scientists have discovered vast deep water gas hydrates in the Arctic that contain abiotic methane, a gas formed by chemical reactions in the crust beneath the seafloor through the process of serpentinisation. Instead of contributing to climate change, these reservoirs could be used to store methane, a lucrative fuel source and principle component in natural gas, which forms when seawater reacts with hot mantle rocks.

A Chelsea defender, Wallace, on loan at Vitesse Arnhem, was arrested in Arnhem, Netherlands on suspicion of a sexual offense and was released without charge. Vitesse immediately excluded him from their squad for the match against Excelsior and fined him, while the nature of the offense is still under investigation. Wallace is a Brazilian Under-20 international and made three appearances for Inter Milan after joining Chelsea in 2013. Vitesse and Chelsea have a close relationship, with Vitesse's owner being an associate of Roman Abramovich. Wallace was pictured playing for Vitesse in February 2023 and made his Chelsea debut during their pre-season tour of Indonesia in 2013.

Cambridge's exam board, OCR, has proposed a new GCSE syllabus for 14-16 year-olds to make learning foreign languages more contemporary and engaging. The draft syllabus includes discussing topics such as tattoos, music festivals, and rap music, as well as reviewing foreign films and tweets, in an effort to replace outdated themes like "my pencil case" and "my family." The aim is to bring back linguistic skills by focusing on grammar and improving student enjoyment and ability.

An officer, Michael Slager, who is white, has been charged with murder after a video emerged showing him shooting and killing an unarmed black man, Walter Scott, as he ran away. The autopsy report confirmed that Scott was struck multiple times in the back and his death was ruled a homicide. The North Charleston Police Department chose to hand the case over to the South Carolina Law Enforcement Division, and the FBI is also investigating. Slager's family and some community members argue that it's irresponsible to label the shooting as racially motivated, while others disagree. Scott's family and local representatives are calling for justice and peace, asking that the legal process be allowed to run its course.

British police helped thwart a suspected terror attack on Anzac Day commemorations in Melbourne, Australia, by tipping off authorities about a 14-year-old boy from Blackburn, Lancashire who was communicating with Australian men about a plan to kill police with knives and swords. Five men were arrested in Melbourne, and a 18-year-old was charged with terror offenses. The Queen and other royals are set to attend Anzac Day services in London, with increased security measures in place following the arrests.

Charlie Hemphrey, a 25-year-old English cricketer who faced repeated rejection in his attempts to breakthrough into the domestic English system, secured a professional contract with Queensland in Australia after an impressive first season, becoming the first Englishman to score a Sheffield Shield hundred for Tasmania since 1978.

A brutal raid by Al-Shabaab militants at Garissa University College in Kenya left nearly 150 people dead and dozens wounded in 2013. The group, which wants to turn Somalia into a fundamentalist Islamic state, claimed responsibility for the attack and has been blamed for numerous attacks in the region and abroad, including the July 2010 suicide bombings in Kampala, Uganda. Al-Shabaab, estimated to control up to 1,000 fighters, has links to al Qaeda and has recruited some Americans, particularly from the Somali-American community in Minnesota. The group is believed to generate tens of millions of dollars annually through extortion, illegal taxation, and other schemes.

In Tikrit, Iraq, mass graves believed to contain the remains of up to 1,700 Iraqi soldiers executed by ISIS in 2014 have been discovered. The bodies, some with hands bound and decomposed, were exhumed as Iraqi forces retook the city, and families gathered to pray and mourn.

Catalans Dragons winger Vincent Duport, a France international and key team member who scored three tries in the first six rounds, has been ruled out for the rest of the Super League XX season due to a ruptured shoulder tendon suffered in a match against Hull FC. Catalans head coach Laurent Frayssinous expressed disappointment for Duport and the team, expressing confidence that he will bounce back stronger from this injury, which follows previous big injuries in his career.

James Hook, the Gloucester fly-half with 77 Wales caps, hopes to showcase his undisputed ability in the European Challenge Cup final against Edinburgh as he aims for a World Cup recall. However, Gareth Anscombe is favored for the utility back role, making it uncertain if Hook will make the final squad despite his experience and versatility at fly-half.

During National Park Week from April 18-26, the United States National Park Service and National Park Foundation are offering free admission to 128 fee-charging parks, including Yosemite and Yellowstone, inviting visitors to enjoy night-time astronomy parties, daytime historical programs, Earth Day celebrations, and family activities. The park service encourages participants to share their experiences using the hashtag #FindYourPark and visit FindYourPark.com for more information. CNN reports on the event, highlighting the giant sequoias and other natural wonders available for exploration during this period.

During his visit to Jamaica, President Obama met Usain Bolt and couldn't resist joining the world-class sprinter in striking his trademark "lightning pose" for a video, which Bolt later shared on Instagram. Obama praised Bolt as the fastest person ever and the pair received a warm welcome from Jamaican Prime Minister Portia Simpson-Miller, who expressed her love for the American president.

The rates of near-sightedness, or myopia, have dramatically increased in East Asia and the West, with up to 96% of 20-year-olds in some countries affected. Researchers believe getting enough sunlight during childhood, when the eyes are still growing, can help prevent myopia, but cultural emphasis on education and long hours of studying make this a challenge to implement.

Controversial wellness blogger Belle Gibson, who gained fame for her inspirational story of cancer survival, has admitted her diagnoses were lies in a recent interview published in the Australian Women's Weekly. Model Jesinta Campbell, who felt let down by Gibson's deception, expressed her disappointment on the Today Show. Gibson's uterus was not affected by cancer, and she has not been diagnosed with any cancer in her blood, liver, or spleen.

Aston Villa midfielder Jack Grealish, who inspired his team to an FA Cup semi-final win over Liverpool, is the latest Premier League starlet to be accused of using nitrous oxide, or "hippy crack," after a selfie of him and a friend inhaling from a balloon emerged. Grealish, who is considering choosing between England and the Republic of Ireland for international duty, joins Liverpool's Raheem Sterling and West Bromwich Albion's Saido Berahino in being caught on camera using the legal high. Ireland boss Martin O'Neill has reportedly given Grealish guarantees to play for the Republic, but the player is leaning towards England.

Liverpool fans are protesting against Hull City's ticket price hike, charging £50 for seats previously sold for £35. Liverpool manager Brendan Rodgers and Hull boss Steve Bruce have expressed support for fans and called for the Premier League to address rising ticket costs, with Bruce urging the league to "remember football belongs to the supporters" and give something back to the "average man in the street."

A remote beach hut on Mudeford Spit in Dorset, with no mains electricity or bathroom, is for sale for £230,000, making it one of Britain's most expensive beach huts. The hut, accessible only by boat or a 'Noddy' train, has a solar panel for lights and a full-size gas cooker, and owners must pay £2,500-£4,000 annual ground rent to the council.

Brazilian supermodel Alessandra Ambrosio, a Victoria's Secret Angel and the eighth highest-earning model according to Forbes, has been signed by Latin America's largest online fashion retailer, Dafiti, to front their new campaign and model their autumn/winter collection. The mother-of-two, who was born in São Paulo, embodies the brand's style and will be joined by Marlon Teixeira, Laura Neiva, Bruna Tenório, Dani Braga, and Ricardo Barbato in the shoot. Ambrosio, who recently attended Coachella and is filming the sequel to Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, will reprise her role as herself in the movie.

In Australia, when Kibulu the African lion cub was rejected by his mother at three weeks old, he was taken in by Donna Wilson at Zambi Wildlife Retreat. Nine months later, the 80 kilogram cub still sleeps most nights in Donna's bed and shares a special bond with her.

A husband wrote an essay celebrating his stay-at-home wife's contributions to their family, admitting he "can't afford" her role due to high child care costs, estimating her annual value at $73,960 based on childcare, cleaning, cooking, and other expenses.

AP McCoy, the 20-time Champion jockey, is being hailed by colleague Andrew Thornton as a unique and dominant force in horse racing, comparing him to Lionel Messi on a horse. McCoy, who will race for the last time at Sandown on Saturday, is known for his unconventional techniques, such as keeping the bit high in the horse's mouth and maintaining control, as well as his exceptional ability to judge pace and find room in races.

A house in Narrabeen, Sydney, which was damaged and at risk of falling during this week's storm, was demolished by a team of firefighters, police, and engineers using unconventional methods to ensure the safety of nearby properties. The storm caused extensive damage across New South Wales, resulting in over 11,000 calls for assistance and $129 million in insurance claims, with the cleanup predicted to take weeks or even months. The demolition was necessary due to the presence of asbestos in the house, and came after the storm resulted in eight deaths and left approximately 2000 people stranded.

A Manhattan real estate publicist and freelance writer, inspired by a traumatic encounter with a sociopath, has been writing to 600 murderers behind bars for six years to understand their motivations. She believes sociopaths crave the feeling of being the smartest person in the room and seek thrills. Notable correspondents include Michael Swango, a doctor who poisoned patients, and Sarah Joe Pender, a woman sentenced to life in prison for murder. Prisoners responded with letters revealing their lack of empathy or remorse, and some disputed being labeled sociopaths. Kreth plans to write a book about her experiences.

Robert De Niro, a Hollywood legend and long-time Democratic supporter, has endorsed Hillary Clinton for president, stating that she has "earned the right" to be elected and that her gender is an important factor. De Niro, who has previously expressed his political views and donated to the Democratic party, was joined by producer Jane Rosenthal in expressing their support for Clinton. In 2012, De Niro hosted a fundraiser for Barack Obama's re-election campaign, but caused controversy with remarks about a potential white First Lady.

Betty Whitehead Willis, the unheralded designer of Las Vegas landmarks such as the "Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas" sign and the Moulin Rouge Hotel, passed away at the age of 91. Known for her gift of neon art, she celebrated her 90th birthday at the Neon Museum in 2013, where her designs are now featured on merchandise from T-shirts to refrigerator magnets.

Russian football team Torpedo Moscow has been ordered to play two home games without fans after they displayed a Nazi symbol during a match against Arsenal Tula. The game was marred by violence and racism, with fans attacking rival supporters and police. Torpedo has been previously punished for monkey chants against opposition players, including Dynamo Moscow's Christopher Samba, and has been fined a total of £17,000 for various offenses. The Russian Football Union has appointed its first anti-racism inspector to address the issue.

Hillary Clinton's presidential campaign will focus on economic security for the middle class and expanding opportunities for working families, while Obama endorsed her as an excellent president. Clinton plans to present herself as a tenacious fighter able to get results and work with various groups. The strategy echoes Obama's 2012 campaign and positions her as an alternative to Republican policies. Obama's endorsement came during a summit of Western Hemisphere leaders in Panama City. Clinton will officially announce her second run for the presidency on Sunday.

The lawyer for Oklahoma reserve deputy Robert Bates, who accidentally shot and killed a man he intended to Taser during an arrest, released documents he claims verify Bates' training records, including one Taser class and several firearms training sessions. The authenticity of the documents could not be independently confirmed by CNN. The documents have become a central issue in the case, with the family of the deceased claiming Bates received preferential treatment due to donations and friendship with the sheriff.

The House In The Sea, a luxurious island retreat in Newquay, Cornwall, can only be reached by crossing a 90ft-high suspension bridge and offers modern design, panoramic sea views, and a fully equipped bar, TV, and billiard table for up to six guests.

A study proposes creating artificial auroras on Earth by firing a high-power particle beam from a space-based accelerator into the atmosphere, potentially producing fantastic colors and even triggering various types of lightning. The Compact Particle Accelerator for Space Science (Compass) in California could make this a reality, offering scientific opportunities to study particle behavior in the upper atmosphere. (Illustration: satellite in orbit)

Golf veteran Graeme McDowell finished six over par at the Masters despite another disappointing performance, insisting he would never skip the tournament despite frustrations. He received a controversial one-shot penalty for moving a marker while swatting a bee, but European Tour chief referee John Paramor later rescinded it. McDowell, a former US Open champion, plans to adapt his putting style to better suit Augusta National.

Dutch winger Memphis Depay, valued at £25million and a target for Manchester United and Liverpool, has held talks with Paris St Germain this week with the Qatari-backed club offering a better financial deal than the Premier League contenders. Depay, who has worked under Louis van Gaal, is intrigued by the prospect of joining PSG and playing with Zlatan Ibrahimovic, and has good relationships with team-mate Gregory van der Wiel and van Gaal's assistant Albert Stuivenberg.

Scientists have found new evidence suggesting that Mars, the bone-dry and seemingly inhospitable red planet, may harbor tiny puddles of salty water at night, according to a new study. While not enough for terrestrial life as we know it, this discovery strengthens the theory that Mars could have conditions for brines to form and raises hope for the existence of past or even present microbial life, making Mars an intriguing next step for humanity's exploration.

Bayern Munich coach Pep Guardiola faces a critical injury situation ahead of Bundesliga and Champions League matches, with Mehdi Benatia's hamstring injury adding to absences of Arjen Robben, Franck Ribery, Bastian Schweinsteiger, David Alaba, and Javi Martinez. Guardiola expressed uncertainty about managing the situation as Bayern needs four more wins to secure the Bundesliga title and visits Eintracht Frankfurt in the league and FC Porto in the Champions League.

A New Hampshire gym teacher, Paul Johnson-Yarosevich, was charged with posing as a young girl on social media and persuading an elementary school student to send inappropriate images of herself, leading to his arrest on Monday on prohibited use of computer charges. The Manchester School District, where he has worked since 2005, placed him on leave and fully cooperated with the police investigation.

Former Republican congresswoman Michele Bachmann drew criticism for comparing President Obama to the co-pilot of the Germanwings plane crash, suggesting Obama is leading the US into disaster with his Iran deal. The comment drew condemnation on Facebook, with some accusing Bachmann of seeking relevance through divisive statements. Bachmann has previously urged Obama to bomb Iran's nuclear facilities and faced dismissal from the president during a White House Christmas Party in 2014.

A new study published in the Health Affairs journal reveals that the US spends $4 billion annually on unnecessary breast cancer treatments and mammograms, with $2.8 billion attributed to false positives and $1.2 billion to overdiagnosis of slow-growing or non-life-threatening tumors in women aged 40 to 59. The researchers, Mei-Sing Ong and Kenneth Mandl, call for re-evaluating current screening practices to reduce overtreatment and its associated costs and risks.

The federal government is investing $4.1 million in a TV drama produced by Put It Out There Pictures, which will be broadcast overseas to deter asylum seekers from entering Australian waters by boat. The drama, which involves the Australian Navy and asylum seekers drowning at sea, will be shown in countries like Syria, Iraq, and Afghanistan, accompanied by a major awareness campaign. However, the Refugee Council of Australia's president Phil Glendenning expressed doubts that the show will discourage asylum seekers from countries like Afghanistan and Syria, and instead suggested the government use the funds to help the UNHCR provide better security for asylum seekers. Prime Minister Tony Abbott maintained that only the Coalition could effectively stop asylum seeker boats, implying that other governments would succumb to pressure from human rights lawyers.

A Facebook post and Craigslist ad matching a rendering of an unidentified body found in Louisiana in 1981 to a missing Michigan teenager, Carol Cole, led to her identification after a 34-year-old cold case. A potential suspect, John Chesson, has been identified based on new information from Carol's family and his own daughter.

Gary Locke, the interim manager of Kilmarnock, has been appointed permanently after a successful seven-game tenure during which he lost only once. Locke, a former Hearts boss, took over from ex-Tynecastle team-mate Allan Johnston in February and will speak at a press conference on Friday after signing a three-year deal at Rugby Park.

Eric Cantona, Manchester United legend, claimed his old side are "kings of the city" again after their derby win and predicted they can win the Premier League next year under Louis van Gaal. Cantona believes United's focus on developing young players in their academy gives them an edge over rivals City in the long run.

A mother-of-twins, Hannah Moore, had her Instagram account deleted after posting pictures of her post-pregnancy stretchmarks to boost her confidence and inspire others. The images were deemed inappropriate and contained nudity and violence, leaving Hannah feeling disgusted and judged. Instagram admitted it was a technical mistake and reinstated her account.
